title: feat(feature/postdetail): M3 Expressive treatment + carousel + floating composer + screenshots
description:
Second half of nubecita-m28.5. Lifts the minimal screen from m28.5.1 into the design intent: container hierarchy, multi-image carousel, floating reply composer, and the full screenshot test harness. Pure UI pass — no data-layer changes.

## Scope

### Container hierarchy (Focus Post emphasis)

- The Focus Post's `PostCard` host gets a `Surface` (or container modifier) with:
  - background = `MaterialTheme.colorScheme.surfaceContainerHigh`
  - shape = 24dp `RoundedCornerShape`
  - default elevation; no custom shadows.
- Ancestors and replies render with the existing `PostCard` defaults on `MaterialTheme.colorScheme.surface`.
- Hierarchy comes from container color + shape — never from typography weight, never from a border, never from a custom shape morph.
- Confirmed via screenshot tests in BOTH light and dark themes (the surfaceContainerHigh ↔ surface delta needs to remain visible in both).

### Multi-image carousel

- The image-embed branch of the existing `EmbedUi` rendering swaps to `androidx.compose.material3.carousel.HorizontalMultiBrowseCarousel` when `images.size > 1`.
- Single-image posts unchanged — keep the existing single-image layout.
- Carousel applies to the Focus Post initially; ancestor + reply multi-image posts can either reuse the carousel or stay collapsed (decide based on what looks best in the screenshot — keep it consistent).

### Floating reply composer button

- Anchored to the bottom of the screen via `Scaffold`'s `floatingActionButton` slot (or equivalent), elevated above the thread content.
- Circle-shaped `Surface` (or M3 expressive `FloatingActionButton` variant if it maps to the design intent's vocabulary cleanly).
- Constructs the composer NavKey shipped by nubecita-8f6.3 and pushes via `LocalMainShellNavState.current.add(...)` — same call site shape as the PostCard reply button so no composer API change.

### Image tap → fullscreen viewer

- Image tap inside the Focus Post (and within carousel slides) emits a navigation effect to the fullscreen media viewer route.
- If the media viewer route doesn't exist yet, file a follow-up bd issue and stub the tap as a no-op for v1; don't block on building a media viewer here.

### Pull-to-refresh

- Wire `PullToRefreshBox` (mirroring FeedScreen's pattern) so the `PostDetailLoadStatus.Refreshing` state from m28.5.1's VM has a UI affordance.

### Tests — full screenshot harness

Compose screenshot tests for:
- focused-post with ancestors
- with-replies
- single-post (no thread)
- blocked-root fallback
- **multi-image carousel** at the focus position (3-image post)
- **container hierarchy contrast**, captured in BOTH light and dark themes — the visual contract that this PR ships

## Out of scope

- Quote-post detail rendering — separate ticket if/when it diverges from PostCard.
- Real-time updates (firehose subscription on the thread).
- Inline composer on the detail screen.
- Custom shape morphing, custom motion graphs, hand-rolled UI primitives — explicitly anti-pattern per the design intent.
- Building the fullscreen media viewer route itself (this PR wires the tap; the viewer ships separately if missing).

## Acceptance

- Focus Post is visually distinguishable from ancestors / replies by container color (`surfaceContainerHigh`) + shape (24dp rounded), confirmed by a screenshot test snapshotting the contrast in both light and dark themes.
- Multi-image posts at the focus position render via `HorizontalMultiBrowseCarousel`.
- Floating circle Surface reply affordance anchored to the bottom; tapping it pushes the composer NavKey.
- ThreadFold.onClick from m28.3's clusters routes here when the focus differs.
- `./gradlew :feature:postdetail:impl:testDebugUnitTest :feature:postdetail:impl:validateDebugScreenshotTest` clean.

Depends on m28.5.1.
close_reason: Merged via PR #102 (commit c46342b) — M3 Expressive treatment + carousel + FAB + media-viewer-tap + screenshot harness all shipped.

title: feat(feature/postdetail): module scaffold + data + VM + minimal loaded screen
description:
First half of nubecita-m28.5. Land the technical foundation — module scaffold, getPostThread data layer, ViewModel state machine, navigation wiring, and a deliberately-minimal screen that just renders the ThreadItem list using existing PostCard with NO M3 Expressive treatment. Polish lives in m28.5.2.

The cut is intentional: this PR proves the data plumbing works end-to-end against a real getPostThread response, ships the route + VM + tests as a complete unit, and leaves a deliberately-bland screen that the second PR replaces. Reviewers can focus on data correctness here, then on visual polish in m28.5.2 without the two concerns leaking into the same diff.

## Scope

### Module (NEW)

- `:feature:postdetail:api` — `PostDetailRoute(uri: AtUri)` NavKey only, per the api/impl convention in CLAUDE.md.
- `:feature:postdetail:impl` — `nubecita.android.feature` convention plugin applied; namespace `net.kikin.nubecita.feature.postdetail.impl`.
- `@MainShell` qualifier on the `EntryProviderInstaller` provider.
- `:app/build.gradle.kts` includes both new modules; `settings.gradle.kts` includes them.

### Data layer

- `PostThreadRepository` interface (likely `internal` to `:feature:postdetail:impl`, promoted to `:core:feed` later if a second consumer arrives).
- `getPostThread(uri, depth, parentHeight)` lexicon call via the existing atproto-kotlin client behind the `XrpcClientProvider.authenticated()` boundary.
- Map `#threadViewPost` (root + ancestors + replies tree) into a sealed `ThreadItem` projection: `Ancestor`, `Focus`, `Reply`, `Fold`, reusing `PostUi` from `:data:models`.
- `#blockedPost` / `#notFoundPost` siblings map to placeholder `ThreadItem` variants (`Blocked`, `NotFound`).

### ViewModel

- `PostDetailViewModel` extending `MviViewModel<PostDetailState, PostDetailEvent, PostDetailEffect>` per the project's MVI convention.
- Per-screen `PostDetailLoadStatus` sealed sum (Idle / InitialLoading / Refreshing / InitialError / NotFound / BlockedRoot) — single field on state, mutually exclusive lifecycle. NOT a flat `isLoading` boolean (per CLAUDE.md's MVI flat-vs-sealed rule).
- Loads on first composition via `PostDetailEvent.Load`, supports `Refresh` (pull-to-refresh handled later in m28.5.2 — VM emits the right state, the screen just doesn't render the refresh affordance yet).
- `PostDetailEffect.NavigateToProfile` emitted on author tap (already routed through the same `LocalMainShellNavState` plumbing the feed uses).

### Render (minimal)

- `PostDetailScreen.kt` — Hilt-aware composable wrapping a stateless `PostDetailScreenContent`.
- Uses a plain `LazyColumn` rendering each `ThreadItem` as the existing `PostCard` (no shape / container-color treatment yet — that's m28.5.2). `Modifier.threadConnector` and `ThreadFold` from `:designsystem` reused as-is for ancestor stitching.
- Standard M3 `TopAppBar` with a back arrow.
- No floating composer, no carousel, no surfaceContainerHigh styling yet — placeholder content density is fine, the screen just needs to render the data correctly.

### Navigation wiring

- PostCard body tap (not avatar / not action row) emits `NavigateTo(PostDetailRoute(post.uri))` via a new `FeedEffect.NavigateToPost` variant — collected in `FeedScreen` and pushed to `LocalMainShellNavState.current.add(...)`.
- Author handle tap continues to route to Profile per the existing m28.3 pattern (no change).
- `ThreadFold.onClick` slot is wired through to `NavigateTo(PostDetailRoute(...))` for the cluster's leaf URI when the focus differs.

### Tests

- ViewModel state-machine unit tests covering each `PostDetailLoadStatus` transition (loading → loaded, loading → error, loaded → refreshing, blocked-root, not-found).
- Mapper unit tests for `#threadViewPost` projection — including `#blockedPost` and `#notFoundPost` siblings.

## Out of scope (deferred to m28.5.2)

- M3 Expressive container hierarchy (`surfaceContainerHigh`, 24dp rounded corner Focus Post shape).
- `HorizontalMultiBrowseCarousel` for multi-image embeds — the minimal screen renders multi-image posts with the existing PostCard image embed treatment.
- Floating reply composer affordance.
- Image tap → fullscreen media viewer wiring.
- Screenshot tests (the visual contract belongs with the visual treatment PR).
- Pull-to-refresh affordance in the screen (VM emits the state; rendering it lands later).

## Acceptance

- Tapping a feed PostCard body opens `PostDetailScreen` and a getPostThread response renders as a `ThreadItem` list.
- All five `PostDetailLoadStatus` variants render correctly (use a debug-only preview / test fixture to flip state).
- Author handle tap routes to Profile.
- ThreadFold tap on a m28.3 cluster routes here.
- `./gradlew :feature:postdetail:impl:testDebugUnitTest` clean.
- `:feature:postdetail:api` and `:feature:postdetail:impl` are in `settings.gradle.kts` and the `:app` dependency graph.
close_reason: Merged via PR #100 (commit d6cc379) — module scaffold + data + VM + minimal screen landed; M3 Expressive treatment continues in nubecita-m28.5.2.

title: Extract Hilt androidTest harness to :core:testing-android module so per-feature instrumentation tests can reuse it
description:
## Why
Current placement (`:app/src/androidTest/` for HiltTestRunner / HiltTestActivity / MockEngineModule / MockEngineHandlerHolder / FixtureLoader, landed via #95 + #96) is structurally wrong: per-Android-docs and Now-in-Android, instrumentation tests should live in the module that owns the code under test. Per-feature tests (`:feature:feed:impl/src/androidTest/`, `:feature:login:impl/src/androidTest/`, `:core:auth/src/androidTest/`) cannot reach harness classes that live in `:app`.

This chore relocates the harness to a new `:core:testing-android` module that all consumers (`:app`, `:feature:*:impl`, `:core:auth`) can depend on via `androidTestImplementation`.

## Scope

**New module `:core:testing-android`:**
- Apply `nubecita.android.library` + Hilt convention plugins. Compose not needed at the module level (consumers bring their own).
- Move from `:app/src/androidTest/java/net/kikin/nubecita/testing/` to `:core:testing-android/src/main/kotlin/net/kikin/nubecita/testing/android/`:
  - `HiltTestRunner.kt`
  - `HiltTestActivity.kt`
  - `MockEngineHandler.kt` (typealias + `MockEngineHandlerHolder`)
  - `MockEngineModule.kt` (the `@TestInstallIn(replaces = [NetworkEngineModule::class])` provider)
  - `FixtureLoader.kt`
- Move `app/src/androidTest/AndroidManifest.xml` to `:core:testing-android/src/main/AndroidManifest.xml` so consumers' androidTest manifest mergers pick up the `HiltTestActivity` registration.
- Update package to reflect new home (`net.kikin.nubecita.testing.android`).

**Convention plugin updates (`build-logic/`):**
- `AndroidLibraryConventionPlugin.kt` and `AndroidApplicationConventionPlugin.kt`: keep `testInstrumentationRunner = "androidx.test.runner.AndroidJUnitRunner"` as the default; modules that opt into Hilt-aware instrumentation tests override locally to `net.kikin.nubecita.testing.android.HiltTestRunner` and add `androidTestImplementation(project(":core:testing-android"))` + `kspAndroidTest(libs.hilt.android.compiler)`.
- Optional: a small `nubecita.android.testing` plugin that bundles the override + deps, applied per-module that opts in. Defer if it doesn't pay for itself yet.

**Consumer updates:**
- `:app/build.gradle.kts`: replace local androidTest harness deps with `androidTestImplementation(project(":core:testing-android"))`. Update `testInstrumentationRunner` FQN to the new package.
- `:app/src/androidTest/java/net/kikin/nubecita/testing/` directory removed entirely (files have moved).
- `:app/src/androidTest/AndroidManifest.xml` removed (manifest merger picks up registration from `:core:testing-android`).

## Verification
- `./gradlew :app:assembleDebugAndroidTest` compiles.
- `ANDROID_SERIAL=<emulator> ./gradlew :app:connectedDebugAndroidTest` runs the existing FirebaseInitTest + MainShellPersistenceTest with no regressions (the broken `listDetailPlaceholder...` test stays broken; that's nubecita-6i9's problem, not this task's).
- `./gradlew spotlessCheck lint` green.

## Out of scope
- The :feature:* and :core:auth androidTest source sets — those land per Phase 1 test task (mqi / 6fd / z9d).
- Fixing nubecita-6i9.
- AGP `testFixtures` migration (deferred until a 2nd consumer of fakes — research recommended waiting).
close_reason: Closed

title: Epic: Android instrumentation testing — Phase 1 harness + reference tests
description:
Stand up a Hilt-aware Android instrumentation test harness for nubecita and land three reference tests (feed render, login intent, token-refresh recovery) that prove the pattern.

## Why
Today only 2 androidTest files exist (FirebaseInitTest, MainShellPersistenceTest). There is no Hilt androidTest plumbing, no Ktor engine swap, and no network-fixture pattern. CI already runs :app:connectedDebugAndroidTest on a cached Pixel 6 / API 35 emulator, so the missing piece is the harness + reference tests.

## Scope (Phase 1)
- Refactor HttpClient DI to allow Ktor engine swap (split engine module).
- Hilt androidTest plumbing: HiltTestRunner, HiltTestActivity, kspAndroidTest.
- MockEngine fixture with a swap-in handler; canned timeline JSON fixture.
- Three reference tests:
  1. FeedScreenInstrumentationTest — pre-seeded session + canned timeline → posts render.
  2. LoginScreenInstrumentationTest — handle input → button enabled → Custom Tab intent fires (Espresso intents).
  3. TokenRefreshInstrumentationTest — stateful MockEngine: 401 → refresh → 200, assert eventual success (KTOR-5681 tolerant).
- Flip CI 'instrumented' job from opt-in label to required-on-PR after a soak window.

## Decisions (from research, plan: ~/.claude/plans/i-want-to-open-cryptic-allen.md)
- MockEngine swapped via Hilt @TestInstallIn (not MockWebServer/WireMock).
- kspAndroidTest for Hilt; mixing KAPT+KSP is unstable.
- createAndroidComposeRule<HiltTestActivity>() with semantics-first assertions; testTag only for parameterized handles.
- Nav3 needs no TestNavHostController — drive nav via rendered semantics.
- Stateful refresh handler, assert eventual success not exact call count.
- Helpers live in :app/src/androidTest/ for Phase 1; extract to :core:testing-android only when 2nd feature module duplicates them.

## Out of scope (Phase 2, file later)
- Like / repost / reply tests → file under existing epic nubecita-8f6.
- Search, profile, settings instrumentation tests.
- Extract harness to a shared module.
- GMD or Firebase Test Lab — defer until CI runtime exceeds ~10 min.

## Effort estimate
~3 focused engineering days for Phase 1 (foundation 1d + three tests 2d), plus ~1hr CI flip after a 3-PR soak. Phase 2 features ~½ day each once the pattern exists.

## Plan
~/.claude/plans/i-want-to-open-cryptic-allen.md

title: feat(feature/postdetail): post detail / thread screen via getPostThread
description:
## Why

Currently the only entry into a post is the feed PostCard. Tapping a post (or wanting to see the thread it sits in) has nowhere to go — there's no detail screen, no thread expander, no way to drill into ancestors or descendants beyond the cross-author cluster m28.3 renders inline. The MainShell's adaptive list-detail scaffold renders a placeholder in the right pane today; without this screen the entire two-pane layout is non-functional. This also blocks:

- **Reply composer entry from a focused post** — nubecita-8f6.3 (reply composer) is shipping with a PostCard-only entry per option D; its composer NavKey is already shaped to be reused by a future PostDetail reply button (no API change required).
- **Self-thread chain rendering past the feed page boundary** — m28.4 detects same-author chains in a feed page; full-thread expansion lives here.
- **`ThreadFold` 'View full thread' tap target** — the fold composable from m28.2 (PR #77) has an `onClick` slot that currently no-ops; PostDetail is its destination.
- **Permalink / deep-link landings** — opening a bsky post URL inside the app should land here.

## Design intent

### Tone

Tactile, fluid, grounded, simple.

### Anti-patterns

- **Not Twitter's flat-card stack.** The focused post must be instantly distinguishable from replies and ancestors without relying on text weight alone.
- **Not a custom-UI nightmare.** No one-off components. Distinct looks come from aggressively utilizing M3 standard shapes, elevations, and dynamic colors — never from hand-rolled primitives.

### Material 3 Expressive — signature moves

1. **The Media Carousel.** Posts with >1 image use `HorizontalMultiBrowseCarousel`. Spring-based scrolling, native to M3, no custom pager.
2. **The Floating Composer.** Reply affordance is a highly-elevated `CircleShape` M3 `Surface` docked to the bottom of the screen, floating above the thread content. Threads-style without the Threads-shape — same idea, M3 vocabulary.
3. **Container hierarchy.** The Focus Post (the post the user tapped into) gets a `surfaceContainerHigh` background and a softer shape (e.g. 24dp rounded corners). Ancestors and replies sit on the standard `surface` background. Hierarchy comes from container color + shape, not from typography weight or border tricks.

### Familiar patterns to preserve

- Tap on an image → fullscreen media viewer.
- Linear thread chronology — ancestors above, focus middle, replies below.
- Standard M3 `TopAppBar` with a simple back arrow.

The design intent locks the screen to **standard Compose APIs** while still feeling distinctly Expressive. No bespoke shape morphing, no custom motion graphs, no out-of-spec elevations — every flourish is a Google-shipped M3 component used deliberately.

## Scope

### Module

- New `:feature:postdetail:api` (`PostDetailRoute(uri: AtUri)` NavKey only) and `:feature:postdetail:impl`. Per the api/impl convention in CLAUDE.md.
- `@MainShell` qualifier on the `EntryProviderInstaller` — this lives inside the main shell, not the outer Splash/Login lifecycle.

### Data layer

- `getPostThread(uri, depth, parentHeight)` lexicon call via the existing atproto-kotlin client, exposed through a `PostThreadRepository` (or extension on the existing feed repo).
- Map `#threadViewPost` (root + ancestors + replies tree) into a UI projection — sealed `ThreadItem` (`Ancestor`, `Focus`, `Reply`, `Fold`) reusing `PostUi` from `:data:models`.
- Handle `#blockedPost` / `#notFoundPost` siblings with placeholder UI.

### Render layer

- `PostDetailScreen.kt` consuming the `ThreadItem` projection. Reuses `PostCard` + `Modifier.threadConnector` + `ThreadFold` from `:designsystem`.
- **Focus Post container**: `surfaceContainerHigh` background, 24dp rounded corner shape, default elevation. Ancestors + replies render with the existing `PostCard` defaults on `surface`.
- **Multi-image embeds**: the image-embed branch of the existing `EmbedUi` rendering swaps to `HorizontalMultiBrowseCarousel` when `images.size > 1`. Single-image posts keep the existing single-image layout.
- **Floating reply composer button**: a circle-shaped `Surface` (or `FloatingActionButton` if the M3 expressive variant maps cleanly) anchored to the bottom of the screen via `Scaffold`'s `floatingActionButton` slot, elevated above the thread content. Constructs the composer NavKey from nubecita-8f6.3 and pushes via `LocalMainShellNavState.current.add(...)` — same call site shape as the PostCard reply button so no composer API change.
- Focused post engagement counts shown expanded; full timestamp visible.

### Navigation wiring

- PostCard tap on the body (not the avatar / not the action row) emits `NavigateTo(PostDetailRoute(post.uri))` via `UiEffect`.
- Author handle tap continues to route to Profile per the existing m28.3 pattern.
- Image tap inside the focus post (and within carousel slides) emits a navigation effect to the fullscreen media viewer route. (If the media viewer route doesn't exist yet, add a follow-up bd issue and stub the tap as a no-op for v1; don't block on building a media viewer here.)

### Tests

- ViewModel state machine (loading / loaded / error / not-found / blocked-root).
- Mapper unit tests for `#threadViewPost` projection.
- Compose screenshot tests:
  - focused-post with ancestors
  - with-replies
  - single-post (no thread)
  - blocked-root fallback
  - **multi-image carousel** (3-image post, focus position)
  - **container hierarchy contrast** (light + dark themes — confirm the `surfaceContainerHigh` ↔ `surface` delta is visible in both)

## Out of scope

- Quote-post detail rendering — separate ticket if/when it diverges from PostCard.
- Real-time updates (firehose subscription on the thread) — initial impl is fetch-on-open + pull-to-refresh.
- Inline composer on the detail screen (reply expands inline rather than navigating to the composer route) — defer; route-based composer ships via 8f6.3.
- Custom shape morphing, custom motion graphs, hand-rolled UI primitives — explicitly anti-pattern per the design intent.

## Acceptance

- Tapping a feed PostCard opens `PostDetailScreen` rendering the focused post centered with collapsed ancestors above and replies below.
- The Focus Post is visually distinguishable from ancestors / replies by container color (`surfaceContainerHigh`) and shape (24dp rounded corners) — confirmed by a screenshot test that snapshots the contrast in both light and dark themes.
- Multi-image posts at the focus position render via `HorizontalMultiBrowseCarousel`; single-image posts unchanged.
- The reply affordance is a floating circle Surface anchored to the bottom of the screen.
- Reply icon on the focused-post block opens the composer (same NavKey shape as the PostCard reply button).
- `ThreadFold.onClick` from m28.3's clusters routes here when the focus differs.
- `./gradlew :feature:postdetail:impl:testDebugUnitTest :feature:postdetail:impl:validateDebugScreenshotTest` clean.
close_reason: Both children shipped: m28.5.1 (PR #100) — module scaffold + data + VM + minimal loaded screen. m28.5.2 (PR #102) — M3 Expressive treatment + carousel + FAB + media-viewer dispatch + full screenshot harness. The parent epic-leaf is satisfied.

title: Like/repost UI: PostCard interaction row + optimistic VM state
description:
Surface like and repost affordances on PostCard with optimistic toggling and Material 3 expressive feedback. Depends on the like/repost data layer issue (sibling under nubecita-8f6).

Scope:
- Extend FeedState (or per-post UI model) with isLiked, isReposted, likeCount, repostCount as flat fields
- Optimistic flip on tap → call repo → on failure, roll back state and emit FeedEffect.ShowError (per MVI conventions: non-sticky snackbar via effect, no Async<T> wrapper)
- Material 3 expressive heart-burst / repost-spin animation hooks on the action buttons
- Local count updates; feed re-fetch is NOT required

Acceptance (per UI test convention):
- VM unit tests cover happy path + rollback on failure (fake repo + Turbine + UnconfinedTestDispatcher)
- Compose previews for liked/unliked × reposted/not-reposted permutations
- Screenshot tests on the post action row

Out of scope: reply (sibling issue), share (sibling issue), like/repost data layer (sibling issue).
close_reason: Closed

title: Like/repost data layer: repository + atproto wiring
description:
Wire repository + remote data source for like / unlike / repost / unrepost record operations against atproto-kotlin. Headless — no UI, no VM, no optimistic state in this issue.

Scope:
- New LikeRepostRepository (or extend existing feed repo — decide in PR)
- createRecord on app.bsky.feed.like with subject = post strong ref; deleteRecord on the returned like record uri
- Same for app.bsky.feed.repost
- Return typed Result<Unit, DataError> via the project's existing error wrapper
- Wire through Hilt module per android-data-layer conventions

Acceptance:
- Unit tests for the data source (Ktor MockEngine) and repository (fake data source)
- No real network in tests
- atproto-kotlin gaps surface as upstream issues at kikin81/atproto-kotlin, not workarounds

Out of scope: optimistic toggle, UI, reply, share. Those are sibling issues under nubecita-8f6.
close_reason: Merged via PR #90 (commit 8377990)

title: Post interactions: like, repost, reply, share
description:
Add user-driven interactions to feed posts so the app stops being read-only. Covers the four primary interaction affordances on a PostCard:

- Like / unlike (createRecord / deleteRecord on app.bsky.feed.like)
- Repost / unrepost (createRecord / deleteRecord on app.bsky.feed.repost)
- Reply (compose flow producing a record with reply.root + reply.parent strong refs)
- Share (Android intent share — link out + cached snapshot, not an at-proto record)

Each interaction needs:
- VM-side state (optimistic toggle for like/repost; full composer flow for reply)
- Repository / data-source wiring against atproto-kotlin
- PostCard / FeedScreen UI affordances + Material 3 expressive animation
- Error effects routed via the existing FooEffect channel
- Unit tests (VM + repo), Compose previews, screenshot tests on UI changes

Reply will likely need a dedicated :feature:compose:impl (or similar) — to be decided in a child issue. Like/repost can ship inside the existing feed feature.

Sub-issues will be created as children of this epic (parent-child dep) and worked on individual short-lived branches off main.

title: Feed UI cleanup: edge-to-edge insets, typography weights, AuthorLine layout
description:
## Why

Visual diff between the production FeedScreen and the design-system reference (openspec/references/design-system/compose/ui/screens/) surfaces four cleanup items. None are net-new features; all are bugs in shipped composables.

## Bugs

### 1. Edge-to-edge insets — content drawing under system bars

`MainActivity` calls `enableEdgeToEdge()` ✓. Manifest has `windowSoftInputMode="adjustResize"` ✓. But content layers don't consume insets:

- `FeedScreen.kt:160-185` Scaffold's `padding` lambda is consumed only by `InitialLoading`'s LazyColumn (line 168). Empty / InitialError / Loaded variants ignore it — visible in the screenshot: status bar overlays the first card.
- `LoadedFeedContent` LazyColumn has no `contentPadding` for Scaffold padding propagation.
- `PullToRefreshBox` indicator draws at the absolute top — under the status bar.
- `FeedEmptyState` / `FeedErrorState` are `fillMaxSize` with no inset awareness.
- `LoginScreen.kt:77-90` has no Scaffold and no `imePadding()` / `safeDrawingPadding()` despite an `OutlinedTextField` — IME would obscure the field on smaller screens.

Goal: maintain edge-to-edge (system bars stay translucent / scrim-free for the killer-feature look) AND give every interactive surface proper safe-area padding.

### 2. Typography rendering all weights as bold

`designsystem/.../Fonts.kt:40-43`:

    val RobotoFlexFontFamily =
        FontFamily(
            Font(resId = R.font.roboto_flex),
        )

Roboto Flex is a **variable** font with a `wght` axis. The single Font entry has no `FontVariation.Settings`, so every `FontWeight.Normal/SemiBold/Bold` call from `Type.kt` maps to the same physical asset rendered at its default wght axis (~700-ish). Result: body text looks bold, headlines look extra-bold, the type scale collapses.

Fix: declare a Font per FontWeight in the FontFamily, each with `variationSettings = FontVariation.Settings(FontVariation.weight(N))`. Honors the variable wght axis.

### 3. AuthorLine row wraps timestamp on long handles

`PostCard.kt:142-160` AuthorLine renders displayName + composite-handle-and-timestamp as two Texts. The composite Text via `R.string.postcard_handle_and_timestamp` ("@%1\$s · %2\$s") has no max-lines or weight, so a long handle pushes the timestamp onto a second visual line.

Reference (`openspec/references/design-system/compose/ui/components/ThreadPost.kt:94-105`) shows the canonical fix: three separate Texts, `Spacer(Modifier.weight(1f))` between handle and timestamp, handle gets `maxLines = 1, overflow = TextOverflow.Ellipsis`.

Splits the `postcard_handle_and_timestamp` string resource into `postcard_handle` and `postcard_relative_time_format`.

### 4. State composables ignore Scaffold padding

`FeedEmptyState`, `FeedErrorState` use `fillMaxSize().padding(...)` only — Scaffold's `innerPadding` from `FeedScreen` is dropped on the floor when the parent dispatches to them.

## Out of scope (separate tracking)

- Self-thread connector line — new capability, separate bd task (sibling under nubecita-m28).
- TopAppBar / bottom nav / FAB and their inset handling — owned by nubecita-cif (App shell epic).
- Status-bar translucent scrim — owned by app shell once the TopAppBar lands.
- Link card embed render (currently shows "Unsupported embed: link card") — owned by nubecita-aku.
- Quoted-post embed render (currently shows "Unsupported embed: quoted post") — owned by nubecita-6vq.

## Process

Drive via openspec change `add-feed-ui-cleanup`. Spec deltas on `feature-feed` (insets) + `design-system` (typography weights, AuthorLine).

## Acceptance

- Cold start of FeedScreen on a 120Hz device: no content draws under the status bar; pull-to-refresh indicator appears below the status bar; last visible card has bottom padding clear of the gesture bar.
- LoginScreen: tapping the handle field opens IME without obscuring the input.
- FeedScreen body text renders at FontWeight.Normal visually distinct from the SemiBold displayName; visual hierarchy of titleMedium / bodyLarge / bodySmall is restored.
- A post with a 30+ char handle (e.g. `someverylonghandle.bsky.social`) still renders displayName + truncated handle + timestamp on ONE line with timestamp pinned right.

title: Upstream fix: DpopAuthProvider.onUnauthorized must handle nonce + token refresh in one 401 cycle
description:
## Background

Confirmed by nubecita-09o: cold start after access token TTL produces FeedError.Unknown ("Something went wrong"). Repro logs show the bug lives in atproto-kotlin's DpopAuthProvider, not in nubecita.

## Bug shape (in atproto-kotlin oauth/.../DpopAuthProvider.kt:67-72)

After idle, the persisted OAuthSession has BOTH an expired access token AND a stale pdsNonce. The first XRPC GET produces a 401 with error=use_dpop_nonce + new dpop-nonce header. onUnauthorized short-circuits on the nonce branch:

    if (newNonce != null && newNonce != pdsNonce) {
        pdsNonce = newNonce; persistNonces(); return true
    }
    return refreshMutex.withLock { refreshTokens() }

The retry uses the new nonce + same expired access token → second 401 with error=invalid_token, exp claim failed. XrpcClient.query only retries once → XrpcError\$Unknown thrown.

User tapping Try again works only because the now-cached pdsNonce means the next 401 has no new nonce to update → onUnauthorized falls through to refreshTokens.

## Scope

Track work to land the upstream fix in atproto-kotlin AND adopt it in nubecita. Out of scope: workarounds in nubecita (e.g. proactive refresh in DefaultXrpcClientProvider) — per user_owns_atproto_kotlin memory, library changes are in scope.

## Tasks

- [ ] File GH issue at kikin81/atproto-kotlin with the trace + proposed fix.
- [ ] Implement upstream fix. Leading candidate: in onUnauthorized, do BOTH recoveries in a single call — update nonce AND inspect the JWT exp claim (or just attempt refresh after nonce update if a fresh nonce was returned alongside an expired-token-shaped 401). Returning true means "I did everything I could; retry once."
- [ ] Cut a new atproto-kotlin release.
- [ ] Bump atproto version in nubecita (gradle/libs.versions.toml).
- [ ] Smoke test: kill app, wait > 1h, cold start. Logcat should show POST <token endpoint> between the 401 and the eventual 200 — no FeedError.Unknown.

## Acceptance

- Cold start after token TTL loads the timeline on the first attempt — no "Something went wrong" screen.
- adb logcat -s AtProtoHttp shows: 401 use_dpop_nonce → POST token endpoint (200) → retry GET (200), all without user interaction.

## Refs

- Diagnostic logs PR: kikin81/nubecita#50 (closes nubecita-09o)
notes: Library bump (PR #51 / commit 66600e1) merged 2026-04-26. atproto = 5.1.2. Issue stays open until nubecita-09o (diagnostic-logs PR #50) merges, because the on-device smoke-test trace in the acceptance criteria requires the AtProtoHttp / FeedRepository log scaffolding to verify the fix end-to-end.
close_reason: Upstream fix shipped: kikin81/atproto-kotlin#34 → atproto 5.1.2; nubecita bumped via PR #51 (commit 66600e1). Diagnostic-logs scaffolding for end-to-end verification merged via PR #50 (commit d05c0bb). On-device smoke test (cold start after >1h idle: GET 401 use_dpop_nonce → POST token endpoint 200 → GET 200, no FeedError.Unknown) is owner-side and not blocking issue closure.

title: Introduce build-logic composite with convention plugins
description:
Extract the repeated Android build-script boilerplate into a composite-build convention-plugin setup (NiA pattern). Creates build-logic/convention/ as an includeBuild sibling with five convention plugins:

- nubecita.android.library — com.android.library, compileSdk/minSdk, JVM 17, consumer-rules.pro, sort-dependencies
- nubecita.android.library.compose — library + kotlin.plugin.compose + Compose BOM + base Compose deps
- nubecita.android.hilt — dagger.hilt.android + ksp + hilt-android runtime + compiler
- nubecita.android.feature — library + compose + hilt + :core:common + :designsystem
- nubecita.android.application — com.android.application + targetSdk + Hilt+Compose for :app

Modules declare alias(libs.plugins.nubecita.android.*) and get all the boilerplate for free. Pure refactor — zero behavior change; ./gradlew build output should match before/after.

Scope: create build-logic composite + five plugins + plugin aliases in libs.versions.toml + migrate :app, :designsystem, :core:auth to use them. Nothing else. Unblocks the feature-login PR on clean foundations.

title: Host OAuth client metadata JSON
description:
Host the AT Protocol OAuth client-metadata.json that Bluesky's authorization server fetches during PAR. Two-step approach: (1) Development: serve from GitHub Pages at https://kikin81.github.io/nubecita/oauth/client-metadata.json via docs/oauth/client-metadata.json in this repo (mirrors the atproto-kotlin sample). (2) Production: swap to a custom domain when launch-ready.

Per atproto-oauth skill: client_id must equal the hosting URL exactly; redirect_uris use single-slash (net.kikin.nubecita:/oauth-redirect — matches the app's applicationId, not the 'com.nubecita.app' placeholder originally sketched on this ticket); application_type=native; dpop_bound_access_tokens=true; token_endpoint_auth_method=none; Content-Type: application/json.

Note: the dev→prod URL swap is a one-way door that invalidates all existing sessions (client_id change). Fine while pre-launch, flag in release notes. GitHub Pages must be enabled for the repo (source: deploy from docs/ on main, or Actions workflow) — currently not configured, this task includes enabling it.

title: fix(feature/feed): wire ThreadCluster.onFoldTap to navigate to PostDetail
description:
## Repro

Open the Feed. Find a `FeedItemUi.ReplyCluster` with `hasEllipsis = true` (cross-author thread cluster with elided intermediate posts). Tap the \"View full thread\" fold.

## Symptom

Nothing happens. The fold is visually a tappable affordance (`clickable(role = Role.Button, onClickLabel = \"View full thread\")`), but tapping it produces no navigation.

## Cause

`:designsystem/component/ThreadCluster.kt` exposes `onFoldTap: () -> Unit = {}` and forwards it to the inner `ThreadFold(onClick = onFoldTap)`. The default is a no-op.

`:feature:feed:impl/FeedScreen.kt`'s `is FeedItemUi.ReplyCluster ->` render branch does NOT pass `onFoldTap`:

```kotlin
is FeedItemUi.ReplyCluster ->
    ThreadCluster(
        root = item.root, parent = item.parent, leaf = item.leaf,
        callbacks = callbacks, hasEllipsis = item.hasEllipsis,
        leafVideoEmbedSlot = videoSlot, leafQuotedVideoEmbedSlot = quotedVideoSlot,
        // onFoldTap not passed → defaults to {}
    )
```

So the fold tap defaults to no-op, dropping the gesture silently.

`CLAUDE.md` and m28.5.1's acceptance criteria explicitly say: *\"ThreadFold tap on a m28.3 cluster routes to PostDetail.\"* The wiring was missed.

## Fix

In FeedScreen's ReplyCluster render branch, wire the fold tap to the same MVI dispatch the body tap uses (the leaf URI is the user's anchor):

```kotlin
is FeedItemUi.ReplyCluster ->
    ThreadCluster(
        root = item.root, parent = item.parent, leaf = item.leaf,
        callbacks = callbacks, hasEllipsis = item.hasEllipsis,
        leafVideoEmbedSlot = videoSlot, leafQuotedVideoEmbedSlot = quotedVideoSlot,
        onFoldTap = { viewModel.handleEvent(FeedEvent.OnPostTapped(item.leaf)) },
    )
```

(The existing `OnPostTapped` event flows through `FeedEffect.NavigateToPost` and lands at `PostDetailRoute(uri)` via `LocalMainShellNavState.add(...)` — the same path PostCard body taps use.)

## Acceptance

- Tapping \"View full thread\" inside a cross-author cluster opens `PostDetailScreen` for the cluster's leaf URI.
- TalkBack continues to announce the fold as a button with label \"View full thread\".
- Existing PostCard body-tap behavior is unchanged.
- A regression test (Compose UI / instrumentation) ideally exists for this — but defer to whatever test surface FeedScreen has at the time of the fix; manual verification on a connected device is acceptable for v1.

title: fix(designsystem): ThreadFold connector line drawn over the dot indicators (drawWithContent order)
description:
## Repro

Open the Feed. Find a `FeedItemUi.ReplyCluster` with `hasEllipsis = true` (a cross-author thread cluster where the wire response surfaced root + parent without intermediate posts). The \"View full thread\" fold renders between the root and parent posts.

## Symptom

The connector line (drawn from `Modifier.threadConnector` and continued through `ThreadFold.drawWithContent`) is rendered ON TOP of the three dot indicators in the fold. The dots are partially or fully obscured by the line — the user sees a continuous solid line where there should be a line-with-dots-passing-through-it.

## Cause

In `:designsystem/component/ThreadFold.kt` (`onFoldTap`-style render), the `drawWithContent` modifier order is:

```kotlin
.drawWithContent {
    drawContent()                       // children (dots + label) drawn FIRST
    val xPx = gutterX.toPx()
    drawLine(connectorColor, ...)       // line drawn LAST → on top
}
```

`drawContent()` runs first, painting the dots. `drawLine` runs after, painting the connector line ON TOP of the dots.

## Fix

Swap the order — line first (background), content second (foreground):

```kotlin
.drawWithContent {
    val xPx = gutterX.toPx()
    drawLine(connectorColor, ...)
    drawContent()
}
```

## Acceptance

- A `ThreadFold` rendered inside a feed `ReplyCluster` shows the three dot indicators clearly visible against (and on top of) the connector line.
- The connector line stays unbroken at the same `gutterX` matching the surrounding `Modifier.threadConnector` calls in `PostCard`.
- The `:designsystem` `ThreadFold` screenshot fixtures regenerate; the regenerated baselines should show the dots ON TOP of the line, NOT the line covering them.

## Out of scope

- Refactoring `ThreadFold` rendering into a different drawing primitive. The fix is a 2-line reorder.

title: fix(app): disable navigation bar contrast scrim so bottom-bar surface extends under the gesture handle
description:
## Repro

Install the app, open Feed (or any screen with the MainShell bottom-nav suite), observe the navigation bar.

## Symptom

The `NavigationSuiteScaffold`'s `surfaceContainer` background stops above the gesture-bar handle (the white pill at the bottom of the screen). A small dark gap is visible between the bottom of the labels (\"Feed / Search / Chats / You\") and the start of the gesture-bar area.

## Cause

`MainActivity.onCreate` calls `enableEdgeToEdge()`. Per the AndroidX `enableEdgeToEdge` API, this sets `window.isNavigationBarContrastEnforced = true` by default, which adds a translucent scrim over the gesture-bar region to enforce contrast. The translucent scrim renders ABOVE the navigation bar's surface fill, creating the visible gap.

Per the `edge-to-edge` skill / Android docs: *\"If any screen uses a `Scaffold` or a `NavigationSuiteScaffold` with a bottom bar (e.g., `BottomAppBar`, `NavigationBar`), set `window.isNavigationBarContrastEnforced = false` in the corresponding Activity for SDK 29+. This prevents the system from adding a translucent background to the navigation bar.\"*

## Fix

In `MainActivity.onCreate`, after `enableEdgeToEdge()`:

```kotlin
if (Build.VERSION.SDK_INT >= 29) {
    window.isNavigationBarContrastEnforced = false
}
```

(`compileSdk = 37`, `minSdk = 28` — the SDK guard handles the API-29-only API.)

## Acceptance

- The navigation suite's `surfaceContainer` color extends fully to the bottom of the screen, including under the gesture-bar handle.
- No visible gap between the nav-suite labels and the bottom edge.
- Existing screen content is unchanged (the navigation bar's vertical extent doesn't change; only the contrast scrim is removed).

## Out of scope

- Other edge-to-edge audits across the app (per the skill's checklist) — file separate issues if needed.

title: feat(feature/feed): scroll-to-top affordance via shared CompositionLocal signal
description:
Scroll-to-top affordance for the Following timeline (and any future feature screen with a long list). Two complementary entry points share one signal contract:

1. **Floating up-arrow FAB** — appears in `FeedScreen` after the user scrolls past N items; tap calls `lazyListState.animateScrollToItem(0)`. Discoverable + matches Bluesky's Android client convention.
2. **Bottom-nav tab re-tap** — when the currently-active tab in MainShell is tapped again, fire a signal that any feature observing it scrolls its list to top. Matches iOS muscle-memory + invisible.

## Architecture (locked)

- **`LocalScrollToTopSignal`** lives in `:core:common:navigation` — a `CompositionLocal<SharedFlow<Unit>>`. Producer-side flow is `MutableSharedFlow<Unit>(replay = 0, extraBufferCapacity = 1, onBufferOverflow = BufferOverflow.DROP_OLDEST)`. The single-slot DROP_OLDEST buffer is load-bearing: rendezvous mode (`buffer = 0`) drops emissions during the LaunchedEffect-restart window between recompositions, which manifests as silently-ignored tab re-taps. Rapid double-taps collapse into one delivered emission (correct UX).
- **Producer**: MainShell's tab-tap handler. When `tappedTab == activeTab`, call `tryEmit(Unit)`. Tab SWITCH does NOT fire — only RE-TAP. Default value is an empty silent flow so previews / screenshot tests don't need to wrap. The `asSharedFlow()` wrapper is `remember`-d so the CompositionLocal value stays stable across recompositions and feature LaunchedEffects don't restart unnecessarily.
- **Consumer**: `FeedScreen` (and future opt-in feature screens). Collect the signal in a `LaunchedEffect` keyed on `(signal, listState)` and call `listState.animateScrollToItem(0)`. The FAB's onClick calls the same scroll function.
- **Visibility**: gated on BOTH `viewState is FeedScreenViewState.Loaded` AND `firstVisibleItemIndex >= SCROLL_TO_TOP_FAB_THRESHOLD` (5). `AnimatedVisibility` with `fadeIn + scaleIn` / `fadeOut + scaleOut`.

## Why this shape

- Producer doesn't know consumers (SharedFlow + CompositionLocal).
- Consumer doesn't know the producer (could be MainShell, could be a test harness).
- ViewModel stays pure — signal is consumed at the screen Composable, NOT in the VM (matches `LocalMainShellNavState` pattern; CLAUDE.md MVI conventions explicitly forbid VMs from accessing CompositionLocals).
- FAB lives entirely in `FeedScreen.kt`. No cross-module surface.

## Module layout

| Module | Adds |
|---|---|
| `:core:common:navigation` | `LocalScrollToTopSignal` (one new file, no new deps) |
| `:app` (MainShell) | Producer wiring inside the existing `CompositionLocalProvider` block |
| `:feature:feed:impl` | Consumer (collector + FAB). One new string resource. |

Future opt-in features (`:feature:profile:impl`, `:feature:search:impl`) repeat the consumer pattern with no new module deps for any of them.

## Out of scope

- Per-feature opt-in beyond Feed. Profile/Search/etc. file their own follow-up tickets.
- Animation tuning beyond the M3 default `animateScrollToItem` (no custom motion graphs).
- A "scroll-to-bottom" inverse affordance (different gesture, different scope).

## Acceptance

- Tapping the bottom-nav tab while already on Feed scrolls Feed to position 0.
- Tapping the bottom-nav tab to SWITCH from another tab to Feed does NOT auto-scroll (Feed restores its last position).

- The FAB is hidden during InitialLoading / Empty / InitialError viewStates regardless of cached scroll position.
- Existing FeedScreen screenshot fixtures (loaded with 3 items; FAB hidden) stay byte-for-byte unchanged.
- New screenshot fixture captures the FAB-visible state (loaded with 10+ items pre-scrolled past the threshold).
- VM unchanged (no new state, events, or effects).

## Refs

Architecture sketched and signed off in conversation 2026-05-03 (post-m28.4 merge). Buffer semantic + viewState gating refined post-manual-smoke during PR #104 review.

title: feat(feature/mediaviewer): fullscreen image viewer route
description:
Required follow-up surfaced by `add-postdetail-m3-expressive-treatment` task 4.5. The post-detail screen wires per-image taps through `PostDetailEffect.NavigateToMediaViewer(postUri, imageIndex)`, but the destination route does not yet exist in `:core:common:navigation`. Until it ships, the screen's effect collector logs a Timber breadcrumb tagged `PostDetailScreen` and surfaces a transient \"Fullscreen viewer coming soon\" Snackbar.

## Scope

- `:feature:mediaviewer:impl` — fullscreen Compose viewer with pinch-to-zoom, swipe-to-dismiss, optional swipe-between-images for the post's full image set, alt-text overlay.
- Wire it into `:core:common:navigation` so the post-detail screen's effect collector can `add(MediaViewerRoute(...))` instead of falling back to the Snackbar.

## Out of scope

## Acceptance

- Tapping ancestor / reply images stays a no-op (per `add-postdetail-m3-expressive-treatment` design Decision 4 / task 4.3).
- The Timber log + Snackbar fallback in `PostDetailScreen.kt` is removed.

## Refs

title: Fix MainShellPersistenceTest.listDetailPlaceholder_survivesMediumToCompactToMediumRotation — LocalConfiguration override no longer drives currentWindowAdaptiveInfo()
description:
## Symptom
`MainShellPersistenceTest.listDetailPlaceholder_survivesMediumToCompactToMediumRotation` fails on phone-class emulators (CI's Pixel 6 / API 35 ~411dp; locally Pixel 10 Pro ~401dp). At the initial "medium width" assertion (line 172) the right-pane placeholder is not composed.

## Root cause
The test overrides `LocalConfiguration.screenWidthDp = 600` to simulate medium width and assumes `currentWindowAdaptiveInfo()` reads from LocalConfiguration. The source comment in the test says exactly this. That assumption no longer holds — the V2 `currentWindowAdaptiveInfo` API (already flagged via deprecation warning in builds) sources from real `WindowMetrics`, not LocalConfiguration. So the override is silently ignored; the strategy sees the emulator's actual width (~400dp = compact), collapses to single-pane, and the placeholder never composes.

## Quick-fix approach (preferred)
Decouple the harness from `currentWindowAdaptiveInfo()` entirely: pass the `PaneScaffoldDirective` (or a fake `WindowAdaptiveInfo`) into `ListDetailHarness` as a parameter. The test constructs the directive it wants for each phase (medium → compact → medium) and passes it through the StateRestorationTester recompositions. No `LocalConfiguration` override needed.

## Out of scope
- Migrating to the V2 `currentWindowAdaptiveInfo` API in production code (separate concern; the deprecation warning will outlive this fix).
- Switching CI to a tablet AVD (rejected — phone profile is intentional, don't spend extra emulator resources).

## Verification
`ANDROID_SERIAL=<emulator> ./gradlew :app:connectedDebugAndroidTest --tests '*MainShellPersistenceTest*'` passes on a phone-class emulator (e.g. Pixel 6 API 35 or Pixel 10 Pro). The companion `mainShellNavState_survivesStateRestoration` test must keep passing — don't regress it.

Discovered while running the existing instrumentation suite on a local Pixel 10 Pro emulator after #95 (HiltTestRunner) merged. The failure is pre-existing — qxu / harness changes did not introduce it.
close_reason: Closed

title: Add TokenRefreshInstrumentationTest with StatefulAuthHandler: 401 → refresh → 200 recovery
description:
## What
Add TokenRefreshInstrumentationTest in `:core:auth/src/androidTest/` exercising the full Ktor Auth refresh flow end-to-end: initial 401 → refresh endpoint → retry succeeds.

## Why :core:auth (not :app or :feature:feed:impl)
The refresh flow IS `:core:auth`'s responsibility. Test it where it lives. This is also the natural home for the OAuth + DPoP fixture work that the feature-level tests (mqi, 6fd) explicitly avoid.

## Scope
- Add an `androidTest` source set to `:core:auth` (depends on `:core:testing-android` from nubecita-87u).
- Create a `StatefulAuthHandler` test fixture (mutable token + counter; matches on path + Authorization header):
  - `401` for stale token on `/xrpc/<authenticated-endpoint>`
  - `200` with new token on `/xrpc/com.atproto.server.refreshSession`
  - `200` with success body when token is fresh
- Pre-seed `OAuthSessionStore` with a valid session (DPoP keys + stale access token + valid refresh token). Use a fake `OAuthSessionStore` via `@TestInstallIn` (in-memory) seeded from a test `SessionFakes` builder. Generate DPoP EC keys at test setup time.
- Test asserts eventual success — KTOR-5681 means the Auth plugin can issue 1 OR 2 refreshes depending on queued-call timing. Assert refreshCount in `1..2`, not exact count.

## Verification
`ANDROID_SERIAL=<emulator> ./gradlew :core:auth:connectedDebugAndroidTest --tests '*TokenRefreshInstrumentationTest*'` passes; rerun 5x locally to check for flakes.

title: Add FeedScreenInstrumentationTest: canned timeline → posts render
description:
## What
Add FeedScreenInstrumentationTest in `:feature:feed:impl/src/androidTest/` (NOT `:app`) that exercises FeedScreen's render path with a fake FeedRepository injected via Hilt's `@TestInstallIn`.

## Why per-feature placement
Per Android docs / Now-in-Android, instrumentation tests live in the module that owns the code under test. Tests in the same module as `internal fun FeedScreen` can call it directly without breaking encapsulation. `:app/src/androidTest/` is reserved for true cross-feature E2E tests (later).

## Scope
- Add an `androidTest` source set to `:feature:feed:impl` (depends on `:core:testing-android` from nubecita-87u for the Hilt test harness).
- Create `feature/feed/impl/src/androidTest/.../FakeFeedRepository.kt` and `FakeLikeRepostRepository.kt`. Fakes return canned `TimelinePage` from a JSON fixture loaded via `FixtureLoader.loadFixture("timeline_basic.json")`.
- Create `feature/feed/impl/src/androidTest/.../TestFeedRepositoryModule.kt` with `@TestInstallIn(replaces = [FeedRepositoryModule::class])` providing the fakes.
- Create `feature/feed/impl/src/androidTest/assets/fixtures/timeline_basic.json` — 3-post canned response.
- Create `feature/feed/impl/src/androidTest/.../FeedScreenInstrumentationTest.kt`:
  - `@HiltAndroidTest`, `createAndroidComposeRule<HiltTestActivity>()`, `HiltAndroidRule` injected.
  - Calls `FeedScreen()` directly inside `composeTestRule.setContent { NubecitaTheme { FeedScreen() } }`.
  - Asserts post text from fixture is visible via `waitUntil { fetchSemanticsNodes().isNotEmpty() }` then `onNodeWithText(...).assertIsDisplayed()`.

## Out of scope
- MockEngine — feature-level tests fake at the repository boundary, not the network layer. MockEngine end-to-end coverage lives in nubecita-z9d (token refresh) under `:core:auth/src/androidTest/`.
- Pull-to-refresh / infinite scroll behavior — keep this test scoped to initial render. Follow-up tests can cover those.
- Pre-seeded OAuthSession / DPoP — never needed here since the repo is faked.

## Verification
`ANDROID_SERIAL=<emulator> ./gradlew :feature:feed:impl:connectedDebugAndroidTest --tests '*FeedScreenInstrumentationTest*'` passes.
notes:
## Bundled scope addition (2026-05-01)

Address Copilot review feedback from #97 in this PR's commit:

1. **`:core:auth` dep scope**: change `api(project(":core:auth"))` → `implementation(project(":core:auth"))` in `core/testing-android/build.gradle.kts`. The `NetworkEngineModule::class` reference in `MockEngineModule` is internal to `:core:testing-android`'s own compilation; consumers' main code already pulls `:core:auth` directly. `api` was leaking ABI without benefit.

2. **Activity dep scope**: replace `api(libs.androidx.activity.compose)` with `api(libs.androidx.activity)` (drop the `-compose` variant). `HiltTestActivity` only extends `ComponentActivity` from `androidx.activity:activity`; bringing Compose runtime in transitively contradicts the module's documented "no Compose" goal. Add `androidx-activity = { module = "androidx.activity:activity", version.ref = "androidxActivity" }` alias to `gradle/libs.versions.toml`. Consumers needing Compose UI testing already declare `androidx.compose.ui.test.junit4` themselves.

After both changes, verify `./gradlew :app:assembleDebugAndroidTest` still compiles and `:app:connectedDebugAndroidTest` still passes — these tests are the existing consumers of the harness.

title: Split HttpClientModule into engine + client modules for test engine swap
description:
Refactor core/auth/.../di/HttpClientModule.kt so the Ktor engine is provided in its own Hilt module (NetworkEngineModule), and HttpClientModule consumes the engine to build the configured HttpClient.

## Why
Today HttpClientModule provides a fully-configured HttpClient with the OkHttp engine baked in. To run instrumentation tests against MockEngine, we need to replace ONLY the engine — not the HttpClient config (timeouts, logging, header sanitization). Splitting the modules lets test code do @TestInstallIn(replaces = [NetworkEngineModule::class]) without losing production HttpClient behavior in tests.

## Scope
- Extract OkHttp engine provider to a new NetworkEngineModule (SingletonComponent).
- HttpClientModule injects HttpClientEngine and builds HttpClient from it.
- All existing unit tests must still pass.

## Out of scope
- Adding any androidTest code (separate task).
- Changing HttpClient config (timeouts, logging, plugins).

## Verification
./gradlew :core:auth:testDebugUnitTest passes. ./gradlew :app:assembleDebug passes.

Refs: nubecita-9tw
close_reason: Closed

title: Same-author thread chain detection in feed mapper (Section B of m28.2)
description:
Spawned from nubecita-m28.2 — Section A (render side: `PostCard.connectAbove`/`connectBelow` + `Modifier.threadConnector` + `ThreadFold`) shipped via PR #77 (primitives) and PR #80 (PostCard parameters), but Section B (detection side) was deferred since it's larger and architecturally distinct.

## Why

The cross-author reply cluster work in PR #80 (m28.3) handles the case where a feed entry has `reply.parent` pointing at a different author. But Bluesky also shows **same-author chains** — when an author replies to themselves multiple times — with a continuous vertical connector through the avatar gutter linking those PostCards. Today, even though `PostCard` accepts `connectAbove`/`connectBelow` flags, nothing in the feed pipeline sets them for same-author chains, so consecutive same-author posts render as visually disconnected cards.

## Scope

- Detect same-author chains in a feed page using `reply.parent.author.did == post.author.did` (or, for chains crossing pages, by looking at adjacent feed entries).
- Set `connectAbove`/`connectBelow` flags on the `Single` (or new) feed-item projection at the mapper layer or in the `FeedViewModel` projection step.
- Decision: collapse non-adjacent siblings into `ThreadFold`, or render every post inline? (Bluesky web defaults to inline + ThreadFold for >2 hops.) Pick during this ticket; document in commit/PR.
- Tests: mapper unit tests for same-author chain detection (consecutive same-author replies → connectAbove/Below set; mixed authors → flags off; chain crossing page boundary).
- Screenshot tests: a feed page with a 3-post same-author chain rendering one continuous vertical connector through the avatar gutters.

## Out of scope

- Cross-author reply trees — done in m28.3.
- Post detail screen thread rendering — separate epic once we have a detail screen.
- Render side primitives — already shipped (PR #77 + PR #80).

## Acceptance

- A `@Preview`-ed feed with three consecutive posts by the same author renders one continuous vertical connector through their avatar gutters.
- The middle post's connector touches avatar borders top + bottom; the first connects only below; the last connects only above.
- `./gradlew :feature:feed:impl:testDebugUnitTest :designsystem:validateDebugScreenshotTest` clean.
close_reason: Merged via PR #103 (commit 4be99e4) — strict same-author chain detection + page-boundary merge with cursor-resync overlap handling shipped.

title: feat(brand): adaptive launcher icon + NubecitaLogo composable + splash/login wiring
description:
## Why

The app currently ships with the default Android Studio green-droid launcher icon. The reference design system at `openspec/references/design-system/assets/` has been carrying brand assets (`app-icon.svg`, `logomark.svg`, `logomark-mono.svg`, `logo.svg`) for the cloud + wordmark identity since the design-system reference was checked in (Apr 24). Time to replace the placeholder with our actual brand and surface the same logo on the system splash screen and the login screen.

The system `installSplashScreen()` call in `MainActivity.kt:41` is also currently a no-op visually because `Theme.Nubecita` (`app/src/main/res/values/themes.xml`) is parented to `android:Theme.Material.Light.NoActionBar` — not `Theme.SplashScreen` — so none of the `windowSplashScreen*` attrs resolve and the platform falls back to defaults. This change fixes that too.

## Reference design

`openspec/references/design-system/assets/`:
- `app-icon.svg` — 512×512 brand mark: solid `#0A7AFF` background, white cloud (3 circles + rounded rect base) centered. Decorative semi-transparent sky-band overlay at top is **dropped** in our adaptive port — see Decisions.
- `logomark.svg` — cloud-only, 72×72 viewport, source for the foreground vector.
- `logomark-mono.svg` — same shape using `currentColor`, source for the monochrome layer (themed icons on Android 13+) and for `NubecitaLogomark` composable tinting.
- `logo.svg` — cloud + "nubecita" wordmark, ~3.3:1 aspect, source for `NubecitaLogo` composable.

`openspec/references/design-system/preview/brand-app-icon.html` confirms the intended treatment ("Android adaptive icon — Foreground + background layers, safe zone respected. Background is brand sky `#0A7AFF`.").

## Scope

### Adaptive launcher icon (`:app`)
- New `res/values/colors.xml` with `@color/brand_sky_blue` = `#0A7AFF` (single source of truth for the brand background).
- Replace `res/drawable/ic_launcher_foreground.xml` — port `logomark.svg` to a 108dp Android vector, white fill, sized within the 72dp adaptive safe zone.
- Replace `res/drawable/ic_launcher_background.xml` — solid `#0A7AFF` rect (no sky-band overlay; see Decisions).
- New `res/drawable/ic_launcher_monochrome.xml` — same cloud silhouette, `#FFFFFF`, ported from `logomark-mono.svg`. The system tints it for themed icons on Android 13+.
- Update `mipmap-anydpi-v26/ic_launcher.xml` so `<monochrome>` points at the dedicated drawable instead of aliasing the foreground.
- Delete the legacy raster icons under `mipmap-{m,h,xh,xxh,xxxh}dpi/ic_launcher*.webp` — vestigial with `minSdk = 28` (every device renders adaptive).

### System SplashScreen API hookup (`:app`)
- Repoint `Theme.Nubecita` parent to `Theme.SplashScreen` (provided by `androidx.core:core-splashscreen`, already a transitive dep — `installSplashScreen()` is in use).
- Set `windowSplashScreenBackground` = `@color/brand_sky_blue`, `windowSplashScreenAnimatedIcon` = `@drawable/ic_launcher_foreground`, `postSplashScreenTheme` = a new `Theme.Nubecita.PostSplash` that takes the original `Material.Light.NoActionBar` parent.
- Result: the system splash now renders the white cloud on `#0A7AFF` instead of the platform default.

### `NubecitaLogo` + `NubecitaLogomark` composables (`:designsystem`)
- New `designsystem/src/main/kotlin/.../component/NubecitaLogo.kt`:
  - `NubecitaLogomark(modifier, tint = MaterialTheme.colorScheme.primary)` — cloud-only mark, square aspect.
  - `NubecitaLogo(modifier, color = MaterialTheme.colorScheme.primary)` — cloud + "nubecita" wordmark, ~3.3:1 aspect.
- Both wrap `Image(painter = painterResource(...), ..., colorFilter = ColorFilter.tint(...))`.
- New vector drawables in `designsystem/src/main/res/drawable/`:
  - `nubecita_logomark.xml` — from `logomark-mono.svg` (currentColor → tinted via `ColorFilter.tint`).
  - `nubecita_logo.xml` — from `logo.svg` (logomark + wordmark), wordmark drawn as paths (no font dependency at render time).
- New `R.string.logomark_content_description` and `R.string.logo_content_description` (value: `"Nubecita"`).

### Wire NubecitaLogo into splash + login
- `app/Navigation.kt:47-49` — replace the empty `Box` in the `Splash` `entry<Splash>` with a centered `NubecitaLogomark` on `MaterialTheme.colorScheme.background`. Eliminates the brief flash of empty surface between system-splash dismiss and the route swap to Login/Main.
- `feature/login/impl/LoginScreen.kt` — insert `NubecitaLogo` above the title `Text` in the centered column. The existing `verticalArrangement = spacedBy(s4, CenterVertically)` accommodates it without restructuring.

## Tests / previews (per the convention)

- `NubecitaLogo.kt` — `@Preview`s for both composables in light + dark.
- `designsystem/.../screenshotTest/.../NubecitaLogoScreenshotTest.kt` — light/dark × {logomark default tint, logo default tint, logomark with custom tint} = ~6 baselines.
- `feature/login/.../screenshotTest/.../LoginScreenScreenshotTest.kt` — existing baselines regenerate to include the logo. No new tests needed.
- `Navigation.kt` Splash branch — covered indirectly by the `NubecitaLogomark` screenshot.
- No unit tests (composables are pure rendering with no logic).

## Decisions

- **Drop the sky-band overlay from `app-icon.svg` in the adaptive port.** Adaptive icons get parallaxed/scaled per-layer by launchers; a fixed band on the background drifts away from the cloud during that motion. Solid `#0A7AFF` is faithful to brand color and looks correct in motion.
- **Delete the legacy mipmap webp's.** `minSdk = 28` always renders the adaptive icon — the dpi-bucketed PNGs are dead code.
- **Wordmark as paths, not text.** The Inter / Material font wiring lives in `:designsystem` but rendering `Text` inside an `Image`-shaped logo composable couples the logo's metrics to font availability. Rasterizing the wordmark as paths in the vector drawable is hermetic.

## Out of scope

- `cloud-illustration.svg` and `empty-cloud.svg` (larger illustrations) — defer to follow-up.
- `compose/icons/*.svg` set in the reference dir — separate task.
- Replacing the system splash with a Compose splash. The current AndroidX `installSplashScreen()` flow stays; we just give it a theme that resolves to the right drawable.

## Acceptance

- Launcher icon on a fresh install renders our brand cloud (white on `#0A7AFF`), adapts to all mask shapes (circle, squircle, teardrop, rounded square), and tints monochrome correctly under Android 13+ themed icons.
- System splash on cold start shows the same cloud on `#0A7AFF` instead of the platform default.
- `LoginScreen` shows the wordmarked `NubecitaLogo` above the title.
- `Splash` route renders the cloud (no flash of empty after system splash dismisses).
- `NubecitaLogo` + `NubecitaLogomark` screenshot baselines added (light + dark, multiple tints).
- `LoginScreen` screenshot baselines regenerated to include the logo.
- `./gradlew spotlessCheck lint testDebugUnitTest validateDebugScreenshotTest` clean.
close_reason: Merged via PR #87 (commit b5a9d4b)

title: feat(feed): render cross-author reply clusters using FeedViewPost.reply
description:
## Why

Replies in the home timeline currently render as standalone posts — the user sees the leaf reply with no context about the thread it belongs to, and has to leave the app or visit the author's profile to figure out what was said before. Observed in production: a Romain Guy reply showed up alone, and the user had to visit bsky.app to see Tom Forsyth's root question + Nolram's intermediate reply to make sense of it.

bsky.app's web client renders this as a **folded thread cluster**: root post → "View full thread" fold (when the chain is longer than 2 levels) → immediate parent → the leaf reply, with connector lines linking the avatars. **All of this is "free" from the feed API** — `app.bsky.feed.defs#feedViewPost.reply` already ships `root`, `parent`, and `grandparentAuthor`. No `getPostThread` call needed.

This ticket renders the cluster in our feed.

## Relationship to existing tickets

- **nubecita-im8** (P3, "Reply-context header"): renders only a single-line "Replying to @handle" header. This ticket is a richer rendering that subsumes im8 (the parent post is rendered inline, so the "Replying to" context is implicit). On merge of this ticket, im8 should be closed as superseded.
- **nubecita-m28.2** (P2, "Self-thread connector line"): renders the avatar-gutter connector for **same-author** chains. The `connectAbove`/`connectBelow` modifier + `ThreadFold` composable from m28.2 are reused unchanged here for the cross-author cluster. This ticket should land alongside or after m28.2.

## Reference design

`openspec/references/design-system/compose/ui/` has all the pieces:
- `components/ThreadPost.kt` — `connectAbove`/`connectBelow` connector via `drawWithContent` at avatar gutter.
- `components/ThreadFold.kt` — "View full thread" fold component.
- `screens/ThreadFeedScreen.kt` — exact rendering pattern for a thread cluster in a feed: `LazyColumn` items dispatched on a sealed `ThreadItem` (`PostItem` carries connector flags; `Fold` carries an elision count).
- `data/FakeData.kt` — fixture for the `ThreadItem` shape.

## Scope

### Data layer (`:data:models`, `feature/feed/impl/.../data/`)

- Extend `PostUi` (or introduce a new `FeedItemUi` sealed type — design as part of this issue) so a single feed entry can render as either:
  - `Single(post)` — unchanged single post.
  - `ReplyCluster(root: PostUi, foldCount: Int?, parent: PostUi, leaf: PostUi)` — three posts + optional elision count.
- Extend `FeedViewPostMapper.kt` to detect `reply != null`, project root/parent into `PostUi` (handle `BlockedPost` / `NotFoundPost` variants by falling back to `Single` + the existing im8-style header for v1; no "Replying to a blocked post" UI yet).
- The `foldCount` indicator: bsky-web renders the fold when there are intermediate posts between root and parent. The feed API doesn't tell us the actual count, but `grandparentAuthor != null && grandparentAuthor.did != root.author.did` is a reliable proxy for "there's at least one post between root and parent." For v1, render the fold whenever the heuristic fires; refine if reviewers want a precise count (would require a `getPostThread` call, out of scope).

### Render layer (`:designsystem`, `feature/feed/impl/.../ui/`)

- Add a `ThreadCluster` composable in `:designsystem` that adapts the reference `ThreadPost` + `ThreadFold` pattern: renders three `PostCard`s with `connectAbove`/`connectBelow` flags set per the reference (root: `connectBelow=true`; parent: `connectAbove=true, connectBelow=true`; leaf: `connectAbove=true`) and an optional `ThreadFold` between root and parent.
- Update `FeedScreen.kt`'s LazyColumn to dispatch on `FeedItemUi`: `Single → PostCard`, `ReplyCluster → ThreadCluster`.
- The connector-line implementation (`drawWithContent` at avatar-gutter x=41dp, 2dp stroke, `colorScheme.outlineVariant`) lands in m28.2 — reuse from there. If m28.2 hasn't merged when this is implemented, copy the code in and reconcile when m28.2 lands.

### Tests

- Mapper unit tests: feed entry with `reply.parent` populated → mapper produces `ReplyCluster`. Entry with `reply.parent` as `BlockedPost` → mapper falls back to `Single` (no exception). Entry with no `reply` → `Single`.
- `ThreadCluster` previews: light + dark theme, with-fold + without-fold variants.
- `ThreadCluster` screenshot tests: 4 baselines (with/without fold × light/dark).
- `FeedScreen` screenshot test: a reply cluster + a standalone post in the same feed page (visual contrast).

## Acceptance

- A feed entry that's a reply renders the cross-author cluster: root post on top, optional "View full thread" fold, parent post, leaf (the entry itself), connected by avatar-gutter lines.
- A feed entry that's not a reply renders unchanged.
- Mapper handles the `BlockedPost` / `NotFoundPost` parent variants without crashing — falls back to standalone rendering.
- Screenshot baselines updated.
- nubecita-im8 closed as superseded (or kept open as the "minimal v0" path if scoping decisions push the cluster work into a follow-up — decision lands during implementation).
- An OpenSpec change is filed if the design touches `data-models` or `feature-feed` specs (likely yes — `PostUi` gains a sum type and `FeedScreen` gains a render arm).

## Non-goals

- Fetching the precise `foldCount` via `getPostThread`. v1 uses the `grandparentAuthor` heuristic.
- Threadgate / postgate awareness ("you can't reply to this thread"). Separate concern.
- Inline expansion of the fold (tap "View full thread" → expand intermediate posts inline). Tapping the fold should navigate to the post-detail screen instead, which renders the full thread.
- Self-thread chains (same author replying to themselves) — covered by nubecita-m28.2.

## References

- Lexicon: `app.bsky.feed.defs#feedViewPost`, `app.bsky.feed.defs#replyRef` — `parent`, `root`, `grandparentAuthor` are the load-bearing fields.
- bsky-web rendering screenshot: in the bd ticket discussion thread (Romain Guy reply with Tom Forsyth root + Nolram parent + fold).
- Reference design: `openspec/references/design-system/compose/ui/screens/ThreadFeedScreen.kt`.

title: feat(app): MainShell composable + NavigationSuiteScaffold + qualifier migrations
description:
## Scope

The meat of the epic. Wire `MainShell` into the outer NavDisplay, host `NavigationSuiteScaffold` over an inner NavDisplay reading `MainShellNavState`, migrate existing feature modules onto qualified bindings, prove process-death persistence, update CLAUDE.md conventions. Partitioned `NavigationEntryPoint`. Compose previews at compact / medium / expanded widths. Screenshot tests for chrome swap (3 widths × 4 selected-state variants = 7 tests).

## Acceptance

- All four tabs reachable; switching preserves per-tab back stacks.
- Cross-tab navigation (tap profile in Feed → push Profile onto Feed tab) works as specified.

- All existing tests green; spotless + lint + checkSortDependencies + actionlint + gitleaks all green.

title: feat(app): adaptive navigation shell — multi-tab MainShell with NavigationSuiteScaffold
description:
## Why

Nubecita's `Main` NavEntry currently renders a placeholder. Three feature epics (search, chats, profile/settings) are blocked on having a top-level navigation surface to land in, and the post-detail epic is sized assuming that surface exists. Building the shell adaptive from day one (NavigationBar ↔ NavigationRail) avoids retrofitting form-factor work per feature.

## Scope

Tracked across three child tickets. Full design at `openspec/changes/add-adaptive-navigation-shell/`:
- `proposal.md` — capabilities + impact + non-goals
- `design.md` — 8 explicit decisions with alternatives
- `specs/app-navigation-shell/spec.md` — 11 new requirements (NEW capability)
- `specs/core-common-navigation/spec.md` — 4 ADDED requirements (MODIFIED capability)
- `tasks.md` — 26 numbered tasks across 8 groups

## Architecture (locked during brainstorm)

- **Layered navigation**: outer `Navigator` (Splash/Login/Main) untouched; inner `MainShellNavState` (Compose-owned, recipe-shape `TopLevelBackStack`) lives for the lifetime of the `Main` NavEntry.
- **Chrome via `NavigationSuiteScaffold`** (M3 adaptive primitive). `NavigationBar` at compact, `NavigationRail` at medium/expanded; drawer mode suppressed.
- **Tab-internal nav routes through `UiEffect.Navigate(target)`**. ViewModels cannot inject `MainShellNavState` (CompositionLocal-only access enforces the MVI boundary).
- **Hilt `@OuterShell` / `@MainShell` qualifiers** partition the existing `EntryProviderInstaller` multibinding.
- **Process-death persistence falls out of the recipe shape** (`rememberSerializable` + `rememberNavBackStack`), closing nubecita-3it as a side effect.

## Children

1. Foundation primitives in `:core:common:navigation` (no UI).
2. Stub `:feature:search:api`, `:feature:chats:api`, `:feature:profile:api` modules (build-only).
3. MainShell composable + chrome integration + qualifier migrations of existing modules + persistence test + docs.

## Acceptance (epic)

All three children closed. Then:
- `openspec validate add-adaptive-navigation-shell --strict` green.
- `openspec archive add-adaptive-navigation-shell -y` cleanly folds spec deltas into `openspec/specs/app-navigation-shell/` (new) and `openspec/specs/core-common-navigation/` (additions).
- Manual smoke on a phone (NavigationBar) and on a foldable/tablet emulator (NavigationRail) shows the chrome swap and per-tab back-stack preservation.
- nubecita-3it closed.

## Non-goals

- Deep linking — separate future epic.
- Settings as its own module — sub-route in `:feature:profile:api`.
- `:feature:search:impl`, `:feature:chats:impl`, `:feature:profile:impl` — only api stubs land here; placeholders in `:app` until each feature's own epic.
- Adaptive list-detail layouts (post-detail epic's child task).
- Refactoring `:core:common:Navigator` from `@Singleton` to `@ActivityRetainedScoped` (separate spec consideration; flag with a follow-up bd issue).
close_reason: All four children closed (8m4.1 + 8m4.2 + 8m4.3 + 8m4.4). MainShell renders on Pixel 10 Pro XL without crash; spec scenarios satisfied; nubecita-3it closed as side-effect.
