title: feat(app): Firebase integration — Analytics, App Distribution, App Check, CI
description:
## Why

Set up Firebase Analytics + App Distribution so internal testers (and eventually beta testers) can install builds without going through the Play Store, and so we have telemetry to inform the 6-day cycle. App Check with Play Integrity is bundled into the same task because the public-repo threat model (anyone can clone the repo + reuse `google-services.json`) makes analytics-pollution defense load-bearing — without App Check, fake events from forks could pollute the dashboard.

## Scope

### Build-side (Gradle)

- Apply two plugins to `:app`:
  - `com.google.gms.google-services`
  - `com.google.firebase.appdistribution` (per the gradle plugin path `com.google.firebase:firebase-appdistribution-gradle-plugin`)
- Add Firebase BoM (`com.google.firebase:firebase-bom`) to centralize Firebase library versions.
- Add dependencies:
  - `com.google.firebase:firebase-analytics` — analytics SDK
  - `com.google.firebase:firebase-appcheck-playintegrity` — App Check with Play Integrity provider
  - `com.google.firebase:firebase-appcheck-debug` — App Check debug provider (gated by `debugImplementation`)
- Commit `app/google-services.json` to the repo. **This is the standard Android+Firebase pattern even for public repos** — the API key in this file is scoped to your package name + registered SHA-1 cert fingerprints, so a fork can't authenticate as your app without your signing keystore.
- Configure App Distribution on the `debug` build type (mirrors slabsnap's setup):

```kotlin
buildTypes {
    debug {
        firebaseAppDistribution {
            groups = "internal-testers"
            releaseNotes = System.getenv("APP_DISTRIBUTION_RELEASE_NOTES") ?: ""
        }
    }
}
```

### App initialization

- In `NubecitaApplication.onCreate()`, initialize App Check before any other Firebase service:

```kotlin
val provider =
    if (BuildConfig.DEBUG) DebugAppCheckProviderFactory.getInstance()
    else PlayIntegrityAppCheckProviderFactory.getInstance()
FirebaseAppCheck.getInstance().installAppCheckProviderFactory(provider)
```

The debug provider issues a placeholder token; register the resulting debug-token fingerprint in the Firebase console under App Check → Apps → Manage debug tokens. Production builds use the Play Integrity provider which Google attests automatically.

- Firebase Analytics is auto-initialized by the SDK; no explicit init needed beyond the plugin + dependency.

### CI / GitHub Actions

- New workflow (or extend `.github/workflows/ci.yaml`) that builds + uploads to App Distribution on push to main (and optionally on PRs from trusted contexts).
- Pattern (from slabsnap's `release.yaml`):

```yaml
- name: Decode service account key
  run: echo "${{ secrets.FIREBASE_SERVICE_ACCOUNT }}" | base64 -d > /tmp/firebase-sa-key.json

- name: Build and distribute
  run: ./gradlew assembleDebug appDistributionUploadDebug
  env:
    GOOGLE_APPLICATION_CREDENTIALS: /tmp/firebase-sa-key.json
    APP_DISTRIBUTION_RELEASE_NOTES: "v${{ needs.release.outputs.new-release-version }} — ${{ github.sha }}"
```

- GitHub Actions secrets to add:
  - `FIREBASE_SERVICE_ACCOUNT` — base64-encoded service account JSON. Generated from the Firebase console: Project Settings → Service accounts → Generate new private key → `base64 -i firebase-sa-key.json | pbcopy` → paste as the secret value.
- The service account needs the **Firebase App Distribution Admin** role (and **Firebase Analytics Viewer** if we ever want to query analytics from CI).
- The service account JSON itself MUST NOT be committed; `.gitignore` already covers `*.json` patterns specific to credentials but verify `firebase-sa-*.json` and similar patterns are excluded.

### Firebase console configuration

- Register two Android SHA-1 cert fingerprints under the project's Android app:
  - **Debug**: `keytool -list -v -keystore ~/.android/debug.keystore -alias androiddebugkey -storepass android -keypass android | grep SHA1`
  - **Release** (when production signing exists): the upload-key SHA-1 (Play App Signing setup) or self-managed release keystore SHA-1.
  - Currently nubecita has no production release keystore — only the debug fingerprint is needed for App Distribution. Release fingerprint is registered when [follow-up production signing ticket] lands.
- Create the `internal-testers` distribution group in Firebase console → App Distribution → Testers & Groups, and add tester emails.
- Enable App Check enforcement for Analytics (and any other Firebase services in use) in the console — without enforcement, App Check tokens are issued but not validated. Recommend enforcement-mode after the first successful production build verifies real-traffic tokens flow.

## Build types & signing

- **Debug builds → App Distribution to internal-testers** (this ticket). Debug-signed; matches slabsnap.
- **Release builds → Play Store** is a separate future ticket. Recommendation: **Play App Signing** (Google holds the app signing key; we manage an upload key only). Lower compromise blast radius than self-managed release signing.
- The existing `-PdebugSignedRelease=true` toggle (added under nubecita-umn for perf verification) keeps working for local `gfxinfo` / Macrobench captures — orthogonal to this work.

## Acceptance

- Pushing a commit to `main` (or running the workflow manually) builds a debug APK, uploads to Firebase App Distribution, and notifies the `internal-testers` group via email.
- Installing the distributed APK on a real device sends Analytics events that appear in the Firebase console's DebugView (with `adb shell setprop debug.firebase.analytics.app net.kikin.nubecita` enabled) within ~minutes.
- App Check tokens are issued (Play Integrity for release, debug token for development). After enforcement is enabled in the console, requests without a valid token are rejected — verify by stripping App Check init from a local build and confirming events stop appearing.
- `google-services.json` is committed to `app/`; the service account JSON is NOT committed.
- The `FIREBASE_SERVICE_ACCOUNT` GitHub Actions secret is set + the workflow successfully decodes it.

## Non-goals (separate future tickets)

- **Production release signing** (Play App Signing setup, release keystore management). App Distribution to internal testers doesn't need it.
- **Crashlytics**. Easy add (`firebase-crashlytics` + plugin) but not in slabsnap's minimum setup.
- **Firebase Performance Monitoring**. Tempting but separate concern.
- **Firebase Remote Config**. Out of scope.
- **Tightening App Check enforcement to BLOCK rather than MONITOR** — recommend starting in monitor mode for a few days to verify legitimate traffic isn't accidentally rejected, then flipping to enforcement.

## References

- slabsnap's setup as the reference implementation:
  - Gradle wiring: `~/code/slabsnap/app/build.gradle.kts` (debug-build-type App Distribution config)
  - CI workflow: `~/code/slabsnap/.github/workflows/release.yaml` (base64-decode-service-account pattern)
  - google-services.json layout: `~/code/slabsnap/app/google-services.json`
- [Firebase App Check with Play Integrity (Android)](https://firebase.google.com/docs/app-check/android/play-integrity-provider)
- [Firebase App Distribution gradle plugin](https://firebase.google.com/docs/app-distribution/android/distribute-gradle)
- [Play App Signing](https://developer.android.com/studio/publish/app-signing#app-signing-google-play) — for the future release-signing ticket.
close_reason: Merged via PR #67 (commit 71697a2)

title: Macrobenchmark module for FeedScreen scroll (120Hz frame timing)
description:
CLAUDE.md declares 120Hz scrolling as a hard requirement, but we have no scripted, reproducible measurement of FeedScreen frame timing. Today we can only eyeball jank via dev-options HWUI bars or 'adb shell dumpsys gfxinfo', which is fine for spot checks but not for regression gating.

Add a ':benchmark' module that uses androidx Macrobenchmark + FrameTimingMetric to script a feed scroll on a real device and produce P50/P90/P95/P99 frame durations + jank counts across N iterations. This becomes the canonical 'is FeedScreen smooth' number we cite when shipping new embed types or rendering changes.

Scope:
- New ':benchmark' Gradle module (AGP test-only module, com.android.test plugin) with macrobenchmark instrumentation runner.
- One macrobench: FeedScreenScrollBenchmark — cold app start, sign in via fixture / mock auth path (or skip auth and seed a test feed), scroll FeedScreen for ~10 fling gestures, record FrameTimingMetric.
- StartupBenchmark as a side benefit (one-line addition once the module is wired).
- README / CLAUDE.md note documenting how to run locally ('./gradlew :benchmark:connectedBenchmarkAndroidTest').

Out of scope (separate issues):
- Wiring macrobench into CI on a hosted emulator (CI emulators are not 120Hz capable, so the numbers won't match device truth — needs a self-hosted device farm decision first).
- JankStats in-app instrumentation for production telemetry.
- Baseline Profile generation (different macrobench mode; valuable but separable).
design:
Module layout: ':benchmark' as a sibling of ':app', applies the 'com.android.test' plugin (NOT 'com.android.application' or 'com.android.library' — Macrobenchmark requires test plugin so it can target a separate APK). targetProjectPath = ':app'. experimentalProperties\["android.experimental.self-instrumenting"\] = true.

Convention plugin: macrobench module is unique enough (test plugin, no production code) that it does NOT fit any of the five existing convention plugins listed in CLAUDE.md. Two options: (a) add a sixth plugin 'nubecita.android.benchmark', or (b) keep the build script self-contained since there's only one macrobench module. Lean toward (b) for now — promote to a plugin only if a second benchmark module appears.

Auth shortcut: macrobench needs a logged-in feed to scroll. Options: (1) a debug-only 'fake auth' flag wired through ':core:auth' that injects a canned OAuthSession (see auth-strategy memory: single OAuthSession persisted), (2) bypass FeedScreen auth gate via UIAutomator clicking through a real OAuth flow (slow, flaky), (3) seed SharedPreferences / DataStore directly from the macrobench setup. Prefer (1) — cleanest and stays in the codebase as a documented testing shim. File a sub-task for the fake-auth wiring before this lands.

Device target: requires a real device (Pixel 6+ recommended for stable 120Hz) connected via adb. Document this in the README run instructions.
acceptance_criteria: ':benchmark' module exists and './gradlew :benchmark:assembleBenchmark' succeeds; FeedScreenScrollBenchmark runs end-to-end on a connected device and emits FrameTimingMetric P50/P90/P95/P99 + jank counts; StartupBenchmark added as a second test in the same module; README has a 'Performance benchmarks' section with the gradle command + device requirements; baseline numbers from the first successful run captured in the issue's notes for future regression comparison.
notes: Filed off the back of nubecita-aku (PostCard external link card embed), where Task 7 only had eyeball-jank verification for the new embed type. Macrobench gives us the number to actually defend the 120Hz requirement claim in CLAUDE.md. Do this before the next embed type (record/recordWithMedia) lands — every new embed adds rendering cost that should be measured against a baseline.

title: PostCard self-thread connector line (drawWithContent at avatar gutter)
description:
## Why

Bluesky shows a vertical 2dp connector through the avatar gutter to visually link consecutive posts in a self-thread (same author, replies in a chain). Reference design at openspec/references/design-system/compose/ui/components/ThreadPost.kt:65-87 shows the canonical pattern:

    Modifier.drawWithContent {
        drawContent()
        val x = 41.dp.toPx()  // 20 gutter + 22 avatar half - 1 line half
        if (connectAbove) drawLine(connectorColor, Offset(x, 0f), Offset(x, avatarTop), 2.dp.toPx())
        if (connectBelow) drawLine(connectorColor, Offset(x, avatarBottom), Offset(x, size.height), 2.dp.toPx())
    }

Plus a `ThreadFold` composable (ThreadPost.kt:174-232) for "View full thread" elision between non-adjacent siblings.

## Scope

Two halves:

### A. Render side (small)

- Add `connectAbove: Boolean = false`, `connectBelow: Boolean = false` parameters to `PostCard`.
- Implement the connector via `drawWithContent` at avatar gutter x=41dp, color = `MaterialTheme.colorScheme.outlineVariant`, 2dp stroke.
- Add `ThreadFold` composable in `:designsystem` matching the reference.
- Screenshot tests for: connectAbove only, connectBelow only, connectAbove + connectBelow, ThreadFold.

### B. Detection side (larger, may need its own task)

- Determine which posts in a feed page belong to the same self-thread (author chain via `reply.parent.author.did == post.author.did`).
- Set the `connectAbove/connectBelow` flags on PostUi at the mapper layer or at the FeedViewModel projection layer.
- Decide: collapse non-adjacent siblings into ThreadFold, or render every post inline?
- Bluesky web defaults to inline + ThreadFold for >2 hops.

## Dependencies

- Render side (A) can land first as a no-op if the FeedViewModel always passes `false` for both flags. Detection side (B) wires the flags to real values.

## Out of scope

- Quoted-post (record embed) rendering — nubecita-6vq.
- Reply-to-other-user thread (the parent post by a different author) — Bluesky shows this with a different visual treatment, separate ticket.
- Post detail screen thread rendering — separate epic once we have a detail screen.

## Acceptance

- A `@Preview`-ed feed with three consecutive posts by the same author renders one continuous vertical connector through their avatar gutters.
- The middle post's connector touches the avatar borders top + bottom; the first post connects only below; the last post connects only above.
- ThreadFold renders three vertical dots + "View full thread · N more" between non-adjacent siblings.
- Existing PostCard tests/screenshots unchanged when both flags default to false.
close_reason: Section A shipped via PR #77 (primitives) + PR #80 (PostCard params + screenshot tests). Section B (same-author detection) spawned as nubecita-m28.4.

title: Tap-to-play with single-player coordinator
description:
## Scope

Wire the actual ExoPlayer playback. The hard part is the coordinator: at most one player instance is materialized at a time, attached to the most-visible video card; scrolling away pauses + releases the binding (player can be reused for the next visible card).

## Design (to be confirmed by nubecita-sbc.2 proposal)

- `FeedVideoPlayerCoordinator` (singleton, scoped to FeedScreen lifecycle): owns one `ExoPlayer` instance.
- `LoadedFeedContent` observes `LazyListState.layoutInfo.visibleItemsInfo`, identifies the topmost video card whose visible-fraction exceeds some threshold (e.g. 0.6), and asks the coordinator to attach the player to that card's `PlayerView`.
- Tap on a video card: emits a `FeedEvent.OnVideoPlay(post)` (or similar) routed through the VM. VM updates `currentlyPlayingPostId` in state. The coordinator binds when a card is in-view AND its post id matches `currentlyPlayingPostId`; binds nothing otherwise.
- Scrolling the playing card off-screen: coordinator pauses + releases the binding; state retains `currentlyPlayingPostId` so scrolling back resumes from the last position.
- Screen exit: `DisposableEffect` releases the ExoPlayer entirely.

## Tasks

- New `FeedVideoPlayerCoordinator` in `:feature:feed:impl` (or a `:core:media` module if nubecita-sbc.1 created one).
- Extend `FeedState` with `currentlyPlayingPostId: String?` (or whatever the design lands on).
- Extend `FeedEvent` with `OnVideoPlay(post)` / `OnVideoPause`.
- Wire the coordinator into `FeedScreen` via `LaunchedEffect(listState) { snapshotFlow { ... }.collect { coord.bindMostVisibleVideo(...) } }`.
- Custom controls overlay: play/pause + mute toggle + (optional) fullscreen affordance.
- Memory + battery test: scroll a feed of 20 videos for 2 minutes, confirm only one decoder active in `adb shell dumpsys media.player`, confirm no player leaks across screen exit.

## Acceptance

- Playing one video and scrolling away pauses it; scrolling another video into view does NOT autoplay (tap-to-play is preserved); only one ExoPlayer instance exists at any time.
- 120Hz scrolling target unchanged with the coordinator active.
- All FeedScreen unit + screenshot tests still pass.
- Manual: feed with 5 video posts plays correctly without ANR or jank.

## Depends on

- nubecita-sbc.1 (deps + smoke test) and nubecita-sbc.3 (thumbnail render) merged first. Strongly recommended: nubecita-sbc.2 (proposal) lands first to lock the design decisions.
notes:
Phase C implementation complete (per openspec change `add-feature-feed-video-embeds`, tasks 4.1-4.7, 5.x except 5.6 archive). Manual smoke (4.8) is the developer's responsibility on a real 120Hz device once this PR merges.

## Files

- `feature/feed/impl/.../video/PlaybackHint.kt` — `enum { None, FocusLost }`. Coordinator-internal hint for the bound card.
- `feature/feed/impl/.../video/VideoBindingTarget.kt` — pure `mostVisibleVideoTarget(layoutInfo, postsById)` function + `VideoBindingTarget(postId, playlistUrl)` data class. Visibility math extracted from FeedScreen so it can be unit-tested in isolation.
- `feature/feed/impl/.../video/FeedVideoPlayerCoordinator.kt` — owns one `ExoPlayer`, scope-cancellable `Mutex`-serialized state mutations, audio-focus contract per spec. Production construction via `createFeedVideoPlayerCoordinator(context, audioManager)` factory; primary constructor takes injected `ExoPlayer + DefaultTrackSelector` so unit tests use relaxed mocks for the player and exercise the audio-focus contract on a mocked `AudioManager` without standing up the real Android framework. Includes `internal` test hooks `notifyBecomingNoisy()` and `handleSystemFocusChange(focusChange)` so tests fire system events without reflecting into AudioFocusRequest (the `getOnAudioFocusChangeListener()` accessor isn't exposed on unit-test Android stubs).
- `feature/feed/impl/.../ui/PostCardVideoEmbed.kt` — phase-C overload `PostCardVideoEmbed(video, postId, coordinator)` that dispatches to a private `PostCardVideoEmbedAutoplay` at runtime and falls back to the phase-B static-poster overload under `LocalInspectionMode.current`. Renders `PlayerSurface` + mute icon + "tap to resume" overlay when bound. Mute icon uses `Icons.AutoMirrored.Outlined.VolumeUp/VolumeOff` (the legacy non-mirrored variants are deprecated). New strings `postcard_video_mute / unmute / resume`.
- `feature/feed/impl/.../FeedScreen.kt` — `LoadedFeedContent` constructs the coordinator via `remember { createFeedVideoPlayerCoordinator(...) }` (skipped under `LocalInspectionMode.current` for layoutlib safety), wires `DisposableEffect(Unit) { onDispose { coordinator.release() } }`, supplies `videoEmbedSlot = { video -> if (coordinator != null) PostCardVideoEmbed(video, postId, coordinator) else PostCardVideoEmbed(video) }`, and runs the scroll-gated bind flow `combine(snapshotFlow { isScrollInProgress }, snapshotFlow { mostVisibleVideoTarget(...) })`.
- `feature/feed/impl/build.gradle.kts` — `testOptions.unitTests.isReturnDefaultValues = true` (otherwise `AudioAttributes.Builder.setUsage` etc. throw "not mocked" against the Android stub jar at coordinator construction time).

## Tests

- `FeedVideoPlayerCoordinatorTest` — 9 audio-focus-contract tests covering all 5 spec scenarios + a few additional edge cases (denied focus request stays muted, release without unmuting doesn't abandon focus, resume after focus loss reacquires + clears hint). Uses `mockkConstructor` to intercept `AudioAttributes.Builder` + `AudioFocusRequest.Builder` chains so the production path constructs successfully against the Android stub jar.
- `MostVisibleVideoTargetTest` — 6 tests for the visibility math (topmost video wins, non-video skipped, threshold edge, unknown keys, empty, viewport clipping).

## Verification

- `./gradlew :app:assembleDebug` — green
- `./gradlew :app:bundleRelease` — green
- `./gradlew :app:lintRelease` — green
- `./gradlew testDebugUnitTest` — green repo-wide (added 15 tests; modified 0)
- `./gradlew :feature:feed:impl:validateDebugScreenshotTest :designsystem:validateDebugScreenshotTest` — green
- `./gradlew spotlessCheck` — green
- `pre-commit run --all-files` — green
- `openspec validate add-feature-feed-video-embeds --strict` — green

## Remaining manual work (task 4.8)

On-device smoke matrix that requires a 120Hz physical device — NOT performable from CI / unit tests. The spec lists 7 scenarios:
1. Music-not-hijacked (Spotify keeps playing while scrolling video posts; pauses on explicit unmute).
2. Single-player invariant via `adb shell dumpsys media.player`.
3. 120Hz scrolling with no jank or decoder errors.
4. Incoming call while unmuted → "tap to resume" affordance.
5. Headphones unplug while unmuted → "tap to resume" affordance.
6. Screen exit → 0 active players in `dumpsys media.player`.
7. Rotation → autoplay restarts muted from position 0.

Plan: run the manual smoke after merge using a real device. Document any findings in this issue's notes; file follow-ups for any divergence.

title: PostCard video embed: thumbnail-only render (no inline playback)
description:
## Scope

Land the video data path through the mapper + PostCard without yet wiring an ExoPlayer. The card shows the static poster (thumbnail), a duration badge, and a centered play affordance. Tapping does nothing yet — the next subtask wires that.

## Why thumbnail-first

Two reasons: (1) it proves the FeedViewPostMapper + EmbedUi extension for `app.bsky.embed.video#view` independently of player wiring (smaller PR, easier review), and (2) it's the only thing that ships if for any reason we revert the player work — feeds with videos still look correct, they just don't play.

## Tasks

- Extend `data/models/EmbedUi` with `data class Video(...)` carrying: poster URL (thumbnail), HLS URL, aspect ratio, duration in seconds, alt text.
- Extend `feature/feed/impl/.../FeedViewPostMapper.kt`: dispatch `app.bsky.embed.video#view` → `EmbedUi.Video(...)`. Pure function + fixture JSON test.
- Extend `designsystem/.../PostCard.kt` (or a sibling `PostCardVideoEmbed.kt`): when `embed is EmbedUi.Video`, render `NubecitaAsyncImage(posterUrl, aspectRatio)` with a centered Material3 outlined "play_arrow" overlay + a duration chip in the bottom-right corner. Tap opens nothing for now (TODO: wire in next subtask).
- Strings: `R.string.post_video_play_label` etc. for accessibility.
- Screenshot tests for the new variant (light/dark, with + without alt text overlay).

## Acceptance

- A feed item with `app.bsky.embed.video` renders the poster card, duration chip, and play affordance.
- Tap is a no-op (placeholder lambda); no crash.
- Mapper unit tests cover (a) well-formed video view, (b) missing thumbnail (poster falls back to gradient placeholder), (c) malformed view returns Unsupported.
- Screenshot baselines committed.

## Out of scope

- Any ExoPlayer integration.
- Tap-to-play.
- Single-player coordinator.

These land in nubecita-sbc.4.
notes:
Phase B implementation complete (per openspec change `add-feature-feed-video-embeds`, tasks 3.1-3.7).

## Spec amendment (mid-flight)

Phase A's lexicon check (in nubecita-sbc.1's notes) found that `app.bsky.embed.video#view` does NOT carry a duration field. The original openspec proposal/design assumed otherwise. This phase amends the openspec deltas accordingly:

- `EmbedUi.Video.durationSeconds: Int?` (was `Int`) — mapper passes `null` in v1; render layer gates the duration chip on non-null.
- `EmbedUi.Video.posterUrl: String?` (was `String`) — render layer falls back to a gradient placeholder when null.
- `EmbedUi.Video.aspectRatio: Float` stays non-null — mapper supplies a 16:9 fallback (`1.777f`) when the lexicon omits it. The render layer needs a stable measurement before the poster loads.

Spec deltas now have explicit scenarios for each branch (well-formed, missing thumbnail, missing aspect ratio, empty playlist).

## Files

- `data/models/.../EmbedUi.kt` — added `Video` variant with the nullable fields above.
- `feature/feed/impl/.../data/FeedViewPostMapper.kt` — `VideoView` arm now calls `toVideoEmbedUi()` which falls through to `Unsupported` when playlist is blank.
- `feature/feed/impl/.../data/FeedViewPostMapperTest.kt` — replaced the "video → Unsupported" assertion with three new tests (well-formed, no-thumbnail, no-aspectRatio, empty-playlist falls through).
- `feature/feed/impl/.../ui/PostCardVideoEmbed.kt` — new file. Phase B variant: poster + optional duration chip only. Aspect ratio applied to outer container BEFORE poster loads. Inline `formatDuration` helper for `m:ss` / `h:mm:ss` (no string resource — digit-only, locale-insensitive).
- `feature/feed/impl/.../FeedScreen.kt` — supplies `videoEmbedSlot = { video -> PostCardVideoEmbed(video) }` to PostCard.
- `designsystem/.../PostCard.kt` — added `videoEmbedSlot` parameter (default no-op); `EmbedSlot` dispatches `EmbedUi.Video` to it. Updated previews — replaced the "unsupported (video)" preview with "unsupported (record-with-media)" and added a video-embed preview using a slot stub.
- `feature/feed/impl/src/screenshotTest/.../ui/PostCardVideoEmbedScreenshotTest.kt` — 4 variants × 2 themes = 8 baselines (with/without poster, short/long duration). Validated.

## Lexicon-duration follow-up

The `formatDuration` code path is dead in v1 (mapper always passes null). It's kept ready for a future phase that sources duration:
1. Bluesky lexicon evolution adds `duration` to `app.bsky.embed.video#view` — mapper trivially populates `durationSeconds`.
2. Phase C (or later) parses the HLS manifest's `EXT-X-PLAYLIST-TYPE: VOD` segment durations after the player loads.

Either way, no spec or composable changes needed beyond the mapper.

## Verification

- `./gradlew :app:assembleDebug` — green
- `./gradlew :app:lintRelease` — green (5 warnings, all baseline-filtered)
- `./gradlew testDebugUnitTest` — green repo-wide (added 3 mapper tests; modified 1)
- `./gradlew :feature:feed:impl:validateDebugScreenshotTest :designsystem:validateDebugScreenshotTest` — green
- `./gradlew spotlessCheck` — green
- `pre-commit run --all-files` — green (includes commitlint + openspec validate --strict)

title: Openspec proposal: video playback strategy in the feed
description:
## Scope

Run the openspec-propose flow for a `add-feature-feed-video-embeds` (or similarly-named) change. Capture the design decisions before any production code lands.

## Decisions to lock down in the proposal

1. **Player lifecycle / instance count.** Single shared `ExoPlayer` attached to the most-visible video card (LazyColumn `visibleItemsInfo` driven), released on screen exit. Or N players (one per card on screen, capped at K). Lean: single player + coordinator. (Cite the bsky-app iOS / Android pattern in the proposal.)
2. **Default playback mode.** Tap-to-play (poster + play button until user taps) vs autoplay-muted-on-Wi-Fi. Lean: tap-to-play for v1 — simpler, no autoplay-policy gating, matches Mastodon/Twitter behavior; revisit autoplay in a v2 once metrics show engagement gap.
3. **Audio focus + mute defaults.** When playing, claim transient audio focus; default unmuted (since user explicitly tapped); mute toggle in the player controls overlay.
4. **HLS adaptive vs fixed.** Use `media3-exoplayer-hls` adaptive selector; in the feed start at the lowest variant and let ABR upgrade. PostDetail screen (separate epic) starts at the user's connection-class default.
5. **Controls surface.** Custom minimal controls (play/pause, mute, fullscreen) over `media3-ui`'s default chrome — chrome looks like a YouTube embed, not a feed-native item.
6. **Error states.** Network failure mid-playback → fall back to thumbnail + retry tap; entire stream missing → render the same poster card without play affordance.
7. **Performance budget.** No more than one decoder active at a time; cards more than N positions away from viewport release their `MediaItem` references; thumbnail bitmap caching coordinated with Coil.

## Process

- Use the `openspec-propose` skill (or `/opsx:propose`).
- The openspec change should produce a spec delta on `feature-feed` adding ~3-4 new requirements about player coordination + render contract.
- Don't archive until the implementation subtasks (thumbnail render + tap-to-play coordinator) merge.

## Acceptance

- Openspec change directory exists at `openspec/changes/add-feature-feed-video-embeds/` (or chosen name) with proposal.md + design.md + tasks.md + specs/feature-feed/spec.md delta.
- `openspec validate <name> --strict` passes.
- Implementation subtasks updated to reference the change name in their notes.
close_reason: openspec proposal landed in PR #56; spec amendments in sbc.3 PR #58

title: Add Media3 / ExoPlayer dependency + smoke-test HLS playback
description:
## Scope

Add Media3 to the dependency catalog and wire the minimum surface area needed to play an HLS stream in a debug Android build. No PostCard integration here — just prove the runtime path works.

## Tasks

- Add to gradle/libs.versions.toml: `media3 = "<latest stable>"`, plus `media3-exoplayer = ...`, `media3-exoplayer-hls = ...`, `media3-ui-compose = ...` (NOT the legacy `media3-ui` artifact — we're using `PlayerSurface`, not `PlayerView`, per Decision 3 in the openspec change `add-feature-feed-video-embeds`).
- Decide module: probably a new `:core:media` Android library (mirrors :core:auth split) so feature modules depend on it via the `nubecita.android.library` convention plugin. Alternative: ship the dep directly from `:feature:feed:impl` and skip the new module — defer until the proposal (sibling subtask) lands.
- Smoke test: a one-off debug `@Composable` that constructs a single `ExoPlayer`, sets a Bluesky HLS URL (e.g. `https://video.bsky.app/...m3u8`), and renders video through the Compose-native `PlayerSurface` from `media3-ui-compose` (file-level `androidx.annotation.OptIn(UnstableApi::class)`). Confirms HLS variant negotiation, Tink + DataStore-DB don't conflict, ProGuard rules in release variant.
- Document version + module placement decision in the bd notes for the parent epic.

## Acceptance

- `./gradlew :app:assembleDebug` succeeds with the new deps.
- HLS smoke composable plays a 480p variant of a sample bsky video on a 120Hz device without dropped frames at app idle.
- ProGuard rules (if needed for ExoPlayer) added to consumer-rules.pro.

## Non-goals

- PostCard integration (separate subtask).
- Single-player coordinator (separate subtask).
notes:
Phase A complete (per openspec change add-feature-feed-video-embeds, tasks 2.1-2.4).

## Decisions
- **Media3 version**: 1.10.0 (matches openspec change pin).
- **Module placement (Decision 6)**: inline in `:feature:feed:impl`. Single v1 consumer (FeedScreen video coordinator + PostCardVideoEmbed both land in `:feature:feed:impl`); `:core:media` can be promoted later if/when PostDetail epic actually needs to share. Avoids pre-emptive module split.
- **Catalog entries** added to `gradle/libs.versions.toml`: `media3-exoplayer`, `media3-exoplayer-hls`, `media3-ui-compose` (NOT the legacy `media3-ui`; using PlayerSurface, not PlayerView).
- **Smoke composable**: `feature/feed/impl/src/main/kotlin/.../ui/FeedVideoSmokeTest.kt` — private `@Preview` exercising the Media3 + media3-ui-compose path. Uses `androidx.annotation.OptIn(UnstableApi::class)` (NOT `kotlin.OptIn`) since Media3's `UnstableApi` is a Java annotation gated by Lint's UnsafeOptInUsageError, not Kotlin's `@RequiresOptIn` mechanism — `kotlin.OptIn` produces a "no effect" warning. **Replaced/deleted in phase C** (sbc.4) by the real coordinator + PostCardVideoEmbed.

## APK / bundle size delta
Baseline (main, no Media3) vs HEAD (Media3 deps + smoke composable). All builds via `./gradlew :app:assembleDebug :app:bundleRelease`.
- Release `.aab`: 18,106,049 → 20,312,814 bytes (+2,206,765 bytes, +2.1 MB).
- Debug `.apk`: 79,417,083 → 86,047,706 bytes (+6,630,623 bytes, +6.5 MB).

The release delta is ~5x the openspec proposal's ~400 KB estimate. Reason: the proposal's estimate assumes R8 minification, but `:app`'s release build type has `isMinifyEnabled = false` (per `AndroidApplicationConventionPlugin.kt`). With minification enabled later, R8 will tree-shake unreferenced Media3 classes and the delta should drop closer to the proposal's prediction. Phase C should re-measure after wiring the real consumer, and we can revisit minification as a separate optimization issue if the unminified delta becomes a concern.

## Lexicon check (task 1.2)
`app.bsky.embed.video#view` is exposed by atproto-kotlin 5.1.2 as `io.github.kikin81.atproto.app.bsky.embed.VideoView` with fields: `alt`, `aspectRatio`, `cid`, `playlist`, `presentation`, `thumbnail`. **No deserializer gap; no upstream issue filed.**

⚠ **Finding for phase B (sbc.3)**: the `view` lexicon does NOT carry a `duration` field (verified at `kikinlex/generator/lexicons/app/bsky/embed/video.json`). The openspec change's proposal/design assumes a "duration in milliseconds" field that doesn't exist in the upstream Bluesky lexicon. Phase B's `EmbedUi.Video` design must either drop `durationSeconds` (no duration chip rendered) or source duration from somewhere else (HLS playlist EXT-X-TARGETDURATION at runtime, after the player loads the manifest). Recommend phase B brainstorm reconciles this — likely path is "no duration chip in v1; revisit if/when Bluesky adds the lexicon field". Tracked here so phase B doesn't re-litigate.

## Task 1.3 (sample HLS URL on-device verification)
Deferred — that's a manual on-device step that the developer (you) does after the deps land. Not blocking the phase A PR. Logging here: when running on a 120Hz device, swap the placeholder URL in `FeedVideoSmokeTest` for a real `https://video.bsky.app/...m3u8` URL pulled from a public bsky video post, then wire the composable into a temporary debug screen (or call it from `MainActivity` under a debug flag) and confirm the HLS variant negotiates + audio is silent + `dumpsys media.player` shows exactly 1 active player.

## Build verification
- `./gradlew :app:assembleDebug` — green
- `./gradlew :app:bundleRelease` — green
- `./gradlew :feature:feed:impl:testDebugUnitTest` — green
- `./gradlew spotlessCheck` — green

title: Epic: Video embeds in feed (app.bsky.embed.video)
description:
## Goal

Render `app.bsky.embed.video#view` payloads inside PostCard. Bluesky encodes feed videos as HLS streams hosted by the user's PDS or the AppView CDN. Today the mapper drops these into the `Unsupported` bucket; users see a placeholder card.

## Why a separate epic, not just a PostCard task

Video adds a runtime dependency (Media3 / ExoPlayer), a non-trivial performance design decision (single-player coordinator vs naive per-card players), a thumbnail-vs-autoplay UX choice, and likely audio-focus + Wi-Fi-only autoplay policies. None of those fit cleanly inside an "add embed variant" PostCard task — they're a feature in their own right.

## Subtasks (created with --parent)

1. Add Media3 / ExoPlayer dependency + verify HLS playback path.
2. Openspec proposal: design exploration for video playback strategy in the feed.
3. PostCard video embed: thumbnail + duration + tap-to-play (no inline playback yet — proves the data path before adding any player).
4. Tap-to-play with single-player coordinator (only one ExoPlayer instance attached to the most-visible video card; others remain at their thumbnail).

## Open questions for the proposal

- One Media3 ExoPlayer instance reused across the LazyColumn vs N instances? (Memory + battery argument is overwhelmingly for one.)
- Inline autoplay (mute) on Wi-Fi only vs always tap-to-play? (Bluesky web autoplays muted; iOS/Android first-party clients differ.)
- HLS adaptive bitrate via `androidx.media3:media3-exoplayer-hls`, or do we hardcode the lowest variant for the feed and let the post-detail screen open at full quality?
- Audio focus / mute defaults: does tapping a video unmute, or is unmute a separate gesture?
- What do we do with videos exceeding some duration (e.g., 60s) in the feed? Same treatment, or escape hatch to "open in detail to play"?

## Out of scope

- Recording / posting videos (post composer is its own epic).
- Live streaming (no AT Proto lexicon for it today).
- Picture-in-picture, casting.

## Acceptance (epic-level)

- A feed item with `app.bsky.embed.video` renders an interactive video card; tapping plays inline; scrolling away pauses; only one player instance is materialized at a time.
- 120hz scrolling target unchanged with N video cards on screen.
close_reason: all subtasks merged via sbc.1, sbc.2, sbc.3, sbc.4 PRs (#57, #56, #58, #59); openspec change archives in this PR

title: Diagnose cold-start 'something went wrong' after token expiry — add OAuth/HTTP/repo logs
description:
## Symptom

After leaving the app closed for a couple of hours, a cold start lands on the FeedScreen full-screen error layout: red ErrorOutline icon + 'Something went wrong / An unexpected error occurred while loading your timeline. / Try again'. Tapping 'Try again' loads the feed correctly on the first attempt.

The icon shown matches FeedError.Unknown (FeedErrorState.kt:103), not Network or Unauthenticated — so the throwable bubbling out of getTimeline is neither IOException nor NoSessionException. adb logcat shows no obvious clue.

## Hypothesis (to confirm with logs, not guess)

DPoP-Nonce + access-token expiry interaction in atproto-kotlin's DpopAuthProvider:

1. After hours idle, the persisted OAuthSession has both an expired accessToken AND a stale pdsNonce (PDS DPoP nonces rotate every few minutes).
2. First getTimeline GET → 401 + new DPoP-Nonce header.
3. DpopAuthProvider.onUnauthorized (DpopAuthProvider.kt:67-72) sees the new nonce, updates pdsNonce, returns true — short-circuiting before the refreshTokens() branch. The expired access token is never refreshed.
4. XrpcClient retries once with the same expired token + fresh nonce → 401 invalid_token.
5. XrpcClient.query() only retries once (XrpcClient.kt:58-67), so the second 401 is mapped to an XrpcException and thrown.
6. DefaultFeedRepository's runCatching surfaces it as FeedError.Unknown.

'Try again' works because the cached XrpcClient + DpopAuthProvider keep the now-correct pdsNonce, so the next 401 has no nonce to update and the onUnauthorized flow falls through to refreshTokens().

## Scope of THIS issue

Add logs only — do NOT fix. Goal: produce a logcat trace that confirms or refutes the hypothesis. Three boundaries instrumented:

1. HTTP boundary (debug-only): install Ktor Logging plugin in core/auth's HttpClientModule at LogLevel.HEADERS, gated on BuildConfig.DEBUG.
2. Auth provider boundary: Log.d in DefaultXrpcClientProvider.authenticated() — cache hit/miss + active DID.
3. Error surface boundary: .onFailure { Log.e } in DefaultFeedRepository.getTimeline() — captures throwable class + message + cause that's currently swallowed into FeedError.Unknown.

The fix (likely upstream in atproto-kotlin's DpopAuthProvider) gets a separate bd issue once logs confirm the path.

## Acceptance

- HTTP request/response logging visible in adb logcat for debug builds, hidden in release builds.
- Repro steps: kill app, wait until access token expires (~hours), cold start. Logcat shows the actual exception identity behind the Unknown error and the request/retry pattern.
close_reason: Merged in PR #50 (commit d05c0bb). Diagnostic logs at three boundaries (HTTP via Ktor Logging gated on BuildConfig.DEBUG with sanitizeHeader, auth provider cache HIT/MISS, repo failure) live in main. Filter via 'adb logcat -s AtProtoHttp FeedRepository XrpcClientProvider AuthRepository'.

title: Introduce LocalClock for deterministic relative-time previews/screenshots
description:
Screenshot tests + Compose previews that include PostCard render the relative-time label (`2h`, `5m`, etc.) by reading `Clock.System.now()` from inside `rememberRelativeTimeText`. As wall-clock advances, the label drifts and screenshot baselines fail validation. The current workaround pins `createdAt = Clock.System.now() - 2.hours` in fixtures so the *gap* is stable, but that:

1. Violates the institutional rule "no `Clock.System.now()` in screenshot tests" (the rule exists for exactly this drift class — it should be impossible by construction, not by convention).
2. Locks every fixture to a single relative-time bucket ("2h"). Tests that want to assert different buckets (`5m`, `10m`, `1h`, `1d`) can't pick them deliberately.

Standard fix: a `LocalClock` `CompositionLocal` defaulting to `Clock.System`. Fixtures use truly fixed `Instant.parse(...)` for `createdAt`. Screenshot harnesses wrap their content in `CompositionLocalProvider(LocalClock provides FakeClock(fixedNow))` and pick `fixedNow - createdAt` to land on whatever bucket they want.
acceptance_criteria:
- New `LocalClock` `CompositionLocal` in `:core:common/.../time/`, default `Clock.System`.
- `rememberRelativeTimeText`'s `clock` parameter defaults to `LocalClock.current` (no-op for production call sites).
- All `previewPosts` / `fixturePosts` helpers in `:feature:feed:impl` use truly fixed `Instant.parse(...)` for `createdAt`.
- All Compose previews + `@PreviewTest` screenshots that include PostCard wrap themselves in `CompositionLocalProvider(LocalClock provides ...)` with a fixed instant for `now`.
- `./gradlew :feature:feed:impl:validateDebugScreenshotTest` green; running it again on a different day still green.
- `:designsystem` PostCard previews + screenshot tests can adopt the same pattern in a follow-on (this ticket scopes `:feature:feed:impl`; the PostCard suite migration is mechanical once LocalClock exists).
notes:
Bundled into PR #48 (the FeedScreen PR) so the PR doesn't ship a violation of the institutional rule. The `Clock.System.now() - 2.hours` workaround currently in the PR will be removed by this same change.

Reference: discussion in PR #48 — institutional rule is 'no Clock.System.now() in screenshot tests because of date drift.' This ticket implements the standard injection pattern that sidesteps the rule by construction.
close_reason: Bundled into PR #48 (commit 4841a63) per its own notes. LocalClock CompositionLocal lives at core/common/src/main/kotlin/.../time/LocalClock.kt; FeedScreen + previews use it via the standard injection pattern; screenshot tests no longer touch Clock.System.now().

title: Route signed-in users to Feed entry (temporary, until app shell lands)
description:
Today `MainActivity`'s reactive routing on `SessionState.SignedIn` calls `navigator.replaceTo(Main)`, which lands the user on the placeholder `MainScreen` (a list of greetings + a Refresh button). Now that `FeedScreen` is a real screen (nubecita-1d5), the post-login destination should be the feed.

This is intentionally a TEMPORARY routing — when the app-shell story (nubecita-cif epic) ships a real shell with bottom nav / top app bar / FAB, the post-login route will land on the shell, and `Feed` will be one of its tabs. Until then, routing directly to `Feed` gives the user a meaningful first screen and unblocks on-device testing.

Scope: one-line edit in `app/src/main/java/.../MainActivity.kt` plus the corresponding `Feed` import.
acceptance_criteria:
- After successful sign-in (cold or warm start), the user lands on `FeedScreen` rather than `MainScreen`.
- Sign-out behavior unchanged (still routes to `Login`).
- `MainScreen` and its VM remain in the codebase (don't delete) — a future change retires them when the app shell takes over.
- No regression in cold-start splash / auth-bootstrap timing.
notes: Part of the nubecita-cif (App shell, splash & onboarding routing) epic. Bundled into PR #48 (the FeedScreen PR) so the feed is testable end-to-end on a device the moment the PR merges.
close_reason: Bundled into PR #48 (commit 4841a63) per its own notes. MainActivity routes SessionState.SignedIn → Feed via navigator.replaceTo(Feed). Temporary destination — superseded once the app-shell epic (nubecita-cif) lands the bottom-nav wrapper, at which point Feed becomes a tab inside the shell.

title: Fix RelativeTimeTest Instant import drift (kotlinx.datetime → kotlin.time)
description:
`core/common/src/test/kotlin/.../time/RelativeTimeTest.kt` imports `kotlinx.datetime.Instant` but the production `formatRelativeTime()` in `RelativeTime.kt` switched to `kotlin.time.Instant` (the stdlib `Instant` promoted in Kotlin 2.x). `./gradlew testDebugUnitTest` currently fails repo-wide on `:core:common:compileDebugUnitTestKotlin` with 12 'argument type mismatch' errors at lines 108–129.

Likely landed unnoticed via a Kotlin version bump after the test was last touched (commit cb589f0 `feat(core-common): add relative-time formatter`).

## Steps to Reproduce

1. Check out main (or any branch with the current Kotlin version).
2. Run `./gradlew :core:common:testDebugUnitTest` (or `./gradlew testDebugUnitTest`).
3. Observe failure: `Argument type mismatch: actual type is 'kotlinx.datetime.Instant', but 'kotlin.time.Instant' was expected` repeated 12 times for RelativeTimeTest.kt lines 108–129.
acceptance_criteria:
- `./gradlew :core:common:testDebugUnitTest` passes.
- `./gradlew testDebugUnitTest` passes repo-wide (no other modules affected by the same drift).
- One-line import change: `import kotlinx.datetime.Instant` → `import kotlin.time.Instant`. `TimeZone` import stays on `kotlinx.datetime`.
notes: Caught while running phase-7 verification on the add-feature-feed-screen branch (nubecita-1d5). Fix bundled into the same PR rather than spun out as a separate cycle — one-line change, unblocks the repo-wide test signal.
close_reason: Already fixed in commit 4841a63 (PR #48 — feat(feed): land production FeedScreen with pagination + pull-to-refresh). RelativeTimeTest.kt:12 imports kotlin.time.Instant; ':core:common:testDebugUnitTest' passes. Issue notes flagged the intent to bundle the fix into that PR — fix landed but issue closure was skipped. Verified on current main.

title: Persist nav back stack across process death (rememberNavBackStack migration)
description:
`DefaultNavigator` currently backs the Nav3 back stack with `mutableStateListOf<NavKey>(start)` (core/common/src/main/kotlin/.../navigation/DefaultNavigator.kt). That state is in-memory only — on process death the back stack reboots from the start destination, so any user-perceived position (deep-linked PostDetail, scrolled-deep Feed, mid-flow Compose draft, etc.) is lost on relaunch.

Per the Nav3 docs (multiple-backstacks recipe: 'persists config changes and process death'), the canonical fix is `rememberNavBackStack`-backed storage with `@Serializable` NavKeys. Our current keys are already compatible: `Splash`, `Main`, `Login`, `Feed` are all `@Serializable data object`. Future keys with arguments (PostDetail with AT-URI, Profile with DID) will need `@Serializable data class` — a per-feature discipline, not a blocker for this change.

This is the prerequisite for relying on `FeedScreen`'s back-nav scroll retention (added in nubecita-1d5) to also survive process death. Today the existing `rememberSaveableStateHolderNavEntryDecorator` + `rememberViewModelStoreNavEntryDecorator` wiring (app/src/main/java/.../Navigation.kt) handles config-change retention correctly; only process death is the gap.
design:
Architectural trade-off requires a decision before implementation. Two viable paths:

(A) **Move back-stack ownership into the MainNavigation composable** using `rememberNavBackStack(Splash)`. `DefaultNavigator` becomes a thin shim that exposes its operations (`goTo`, `goBack`, `replaceTo`) over a Compose-side reference handed in at composition time, OR the Navigator interface migrates to a `@Composable`-context provider that MainActivity reads via `LocalNavigator.current`. This is the canonical Nav3 shape per the recipes but breaks our current pattern of `@Inject lateinit var navigator: Navigator` in MainActivity reactive collectors.

(B) **Keep Hilt-singleton `DefaultNavigator` and add SavedStateHandle-backed persistence inside the impl.** Inject `SavedStateHandle` (via a `@HiltViewModel`-scoped wrapper, or via a custom `@Singleton` saved-state surface), serialize the `SnapshotStateList<NavKey>` to JSON on each mutation, restore on construction. Preserves all existing call sites; trades architectural purity for compatibility.

Lean: A is the documented Nav3 idiom. B preserves MainActivity's existing reactive routing without restructuring. Decide in implementation step 1.

Out of scope: ViewModel-level `SavedStateHandle` plumbing for per-screen state survival (e.g., `FeedState.posts`). That's a separate per-feature concern; the back stack is the only thing this ticket persists.
acceptance_criteria:
- Back stack survives process death: scroll-deep + kill process via `adb shell am kill` + relaunch lands on the deep destination, not the start.
- Existing reactive routing in `MainActivity` (`sessionStateProvider.state.collect → navigator.replaceTo`) keeps working with no behavior change for cold-start auth resolution.
- Existing per-entry `rememberSaveable` state retention (config change, push/pop) continues to round-trip — no regression.
- No source-level breakage at `Navigator` call sites in `:feature:*:impl` modules (login, feed); if the chosen path requires interface changes, callers update in the same change.
- nav3-core stays at 1.1.1 (no version bump required by the migration itself).
- Test coverage: at minimum a unit test of the serialize/restore round-trip; an instrumented smoke test using `Don't keep activities` developer-option semantics (or `ActivityScenario`'s state-machine APIs) if practical.
notes:
Path-A reference: navigation-3 skill multiple-backstacks recipe — uses `rememberNavigationState` at the composable layer with `rememberNavBackStack(key)` per top-level route.

Path-B reference: standard Hilt + SavedStateHandle pattern. Note that `SavedStateHandle` is normally VM-scoped; using it inside a `@Singleton` requires injecting it from a top-level retained surface (e.g., `MainActivity`'s `savedStateRegistryOwner`) and threading it explicitly.

Risk: NavKey serialization regression. Once the back stack is persisted, every NavKey type that lands on it must be `@Serializable` and remain backward-compatible across versions (renames break restore). Add a lint or unit test that enumerates all NavKey subclasses and asserts `@Serializable` presence.

Coordination: tasks in nubecita-1d5 (`add-feature-feed-screen`) currently mention `SavedStateHandle` for process-death scroll retention. Once this ticket lands, that language can simplify — `rememberSaveable` + persisted back stack covers both config change and process death without per-screen `SavedStateHandle` plumbing for the scroll-position case specifically.

Reference: openspec change `add-feature-feed-screen` design audit findings (the audit flagged this gap and recommended filing this follow-on).
close_reason: Process-death persistence verified by MainShellPersistenceTest in PR #73 (closes-via spec scenario in app-navigation-shell + the rememberSerializable + rememberNavBackStack adoption from nubecita-8m4.1)

title: Add accessibility labels to PostStat icons (especially the share button)
description:
PostStat icons currently use Icon(contentDescription = null) per the convention that the visible count text is the announced label. That's correct for reply/repost/like (where the count is shown), but the share button passes count = "" so TalkBack announces nothing — the user has no way to discover the action exists.

Fix shape:
  - Pass an accessibility-only label per stat: replyLabel/repostLabel/likeLabel/shareLabel as parameters to PostStat, default to null (Icon(contentDescription = null) preserved).
  - PostCard wires R.string.postcard_action_reply / _repost / _like / _share strings as the labels.
  - For the share button specifically, contentDescription becomes the SOURCE of the announcement since count is empty.

Also worth checking: when active = true on like/repost, the announcement should reflect the active state ("Unlike" / "Undo repost" rather than "Like" / "Repost"). Add separate strings + flip based on active.

Surfaced during the hardcoded-strings audit on PR #42 — most user-facing strings got localized, but PostStat doesn't expose ANY strings (the count is its only label, and counts aren't translatable text). Accessibility is the under-the-hood concern this surfaces.

Also worth: the icon labels are currently brand-implicit (heart = like, repeat = repost). On voice-only navigation the action verbs need to be explicit.
acceptance_criteria: PostStat exposes a label parameter per call site; PostCard supplies localized labels via R.string.postcard_action_*; share-button TalkBack announcement reads the action name; like/repost active state announces the inverse action; new strings in :designsystem/strings.xml; existing screenshot baselines unchanged (no visual diff).
close_reason: Merged in PR #55 (commit fd6d942). PostStat now requires accessibilityLabel; one-shot actions (reply, share) use Modifier.clickable + Role.Button + onClickLabel; toggles (like, repost) use Modifier.toggleable + Role.Switch + Icon.contentDescription so TalkBack announces both action AND on/off state. New strings_action_reply/_repost/_like; existing _share retained.

title: PostCard quoted post (record) embed (app.bsky.embed.record)
description:
Render Bluesky quoted-post embeds in PostCard. Schema (per app.bsky.embed.record lexicon): a referenced app.bsky.feed.defs#postView containing the quoted post's full author + text + embed.

UX shape: nested card-within-card. Common implementations use a compact density (smaller avatar, no action row, single-line truncated text) or full density (same as parent PostCard). Choose at implementation time — likely compact, with tap-to-expand or tap-to-navigate-to-detail.

Scope decision context: filed under nubecita-6rh as v2 — lands after PostCard v1 (text + images) and v1.1 (external) ship, AND after feed scrolling is verified at 120Hz on a known-good device. Recursive composable + new layout decisions make this the highest-architectural-risk embed.

Implementation notes:
- Mapper sealed type adds Record(quotedPost: QuotedPostUiModel) variant.
- QuotedPostUiModel reuses author + text + (one level of) embed but with compact/disabled action row.
- Recursive PostCard problem: limit nesting to 1 level (a quoted post inside a quoted post just renders 'Quoted post (tap to view)' chip).
- Tap behavior: navigate to PostDetail screen (separate ticket — likely a future epic) or open the quoted post in-feed scroll.

Decision doc: docs/superpowers/specs/2026-04-25-postcard-embed-scope-v1.md
acceptance_criteria: Record(quotedPost) variant added to embed sealed type; nested card composable renders at compact density (≤ parent height); recursive nesting is bounded to 1 level (deeper renders a 'View thread' chip); tap navigates to a destination (post detail or in-feed scroll — pick at implementation); unit test covers nested + bounded-recursion cases.
close_reason: Shipped via PR #62 (squash-merged as 7d4909f). All 37 OpenSpec tasks complete; 120 Hz scroll target verified on Pixel 10 Pro XL (0.22% jank rate, 95th percentile = 7ms under the 8.33ms budget). Two rounds of Copilot review threads resolved. OpenSpec change at openspec/changes/add-feature-feed-record-embed/ pending archive in a follow-up.

title: PostCard external link card embed (app.bsky.embed.external)
description:
Render Bluesky external link card embeds in PostCard. Schema (per app.bsky.embed.external lexicon):
- uri: String (the linked URL)
- title: String (page title, may be empty)
- description: String (page description, may be empty)
- thumb: Blob? (optional preview image; blob ref to fetch via getBlob)

UX shape: Material 3 surface tile with rounded corners, thumb on left or top (decide based on aspect ratio), title (titleMedium), description (bodySmall, ellipsized at 2 lines), domain footer (labelSmall). Tap opens via custom tab (use existing CustomTabsIntent path from MainActivity OAuth flow as the pattern).

Scope decision context: filed under nubecita-6rh as v1.1 — next embed type to land after PostCard v1 (text + images) ships. Common in news/article-sharing feeds.

Implementation notes:
- Mapper sealed type adds External(uri, title, description, thumbUrl?) variant.
- Thumb blob ref: AT Protocol blobs need to be turned into a CDN URL (typically https://cdn.bsky.app/img/feed_thumbnail/plain/{did}/{cid}@jpeg or similar — verify against atproto-kotlin or a working post fixture).
- Reuse NubecitaAsyncImage for the thumb.
- Domain extraction: Uri.parse(uri).host with www. stripped.

Decision doc: docs/superpowers/specs/2026-04-25-postcard-embed-scope-v1.md
acceptance_criteria: External(uri, title, description, thumbUrl?) variant added to embed sealed type; PostCardExternalEmbed composable renders the card with M3 Expressive surface treatment; tap launches CustomTabsIntent; missing-thumb case renders gracefully (text-only card); unit test covers the mapper for fixture posts with and without thumb.
notes:
Render the link card NATIVELY in Compose — no WebView. The `app.bsky.embed.external#view` payload already carries title, description, thumbnail, URI, and source domain. The card should:

- Surface thumbnail (NubecitaAsyncImage) at the top of the card, with a fixed aspect ratio (probably 1.91:1 / OG image standard).
- Title + description below the thumbnail (truncate to 2-3 lines).
- Domain hint in a small footer row (host of the URI; visually distinct from the body copy).
- Tap behavior: open the URI via Custom Tabs (NOT WebView, NOT Browser intent — Custom Tabs gives the user the back-affordance + theme handoff).

Avoid WebView entirely. Reasons:
1. WebView is heavy (40+ MB process, separate renderer thread, memory leaks if not lifecycle-managed).
2. Bluesky's link cards are static OG-style metadata previews, not embedded interactive iframes — there's no rendering need for HTML.
3. Custom Tabs gives the user a familiar, fast, themable tap-through that respects their default browser.

Future enhancement (separate ticket): video URLs (YouTube, Vimeo) detected in `external.uri` could swap to an inline player, but v1 just renders the static card and opens the URI in Custom Tabs on tap.
close_reason: Closed

title: Decision: Bluesky embed types supported in PostCard v1
description:
Bluesky posts may carry one of five embed types (or none):
1. app.bsky.embed.images — 1-4 images, each with alt text, aspect ratio, blob ref
2. app.bsky.embed.external — link card (uri, title, description, optional thumb)
3. app.bsky.embed.record — quoted post (a referenced post view)
4. app.bsky.embed.video — single video clip with playlist (.m3u8) URL
5. app.bsky.embed.recordWithMedia — composition of #record + #images or #video

Each adds material UI work and dependency surface (video needs Media3/ExoPlayer, quoted posts need recursive PostCard rendering, link cards need OG-preview-style rendering). Shipping all five as v1 inflates feed scope significantly.

Recommendation:
- v1: text + #images. Highest-value, lowest-effort. Covers majority of feed posts.
- v1.1: #external. Modest effort, common in news/article-sharing feeds.
- v2: #record (quoted posts). Recursive composable + new layout work.
- v3: #video, #recordWithMedia. ExoPlayer integration is a multi-day item.

Decide here, document in PostCard composable's KDoc which embed types it handles vs. degrades to an 'Unsupported embed' chip, and file follow-on bd issues for each deferred embed type so they're not lost.

Blocks PostCard (nubecita-w0d) — embed handling is the bulk of PostCard surface area.
design: DECISION: PostCard v1 renders text + app.bsky.embed.images only. All other embed types degrade to an 'Unsupported embed' chip (deliberate degradation, not error). Roadmap: v1.1=external (nubecita-aku), v2=record (nubecita-6vq), v3=video+recordWithMedia (nubecita-xsu, nubecita-umn). Full rationale + UX shape + migration triggers in docs/superpowers/specs/2026-04-25-postcard-embed-scope-v1.md.
acceptance_criteria: Decision recorded in this issue (or a docs/superpowers/specs/ note); follow-on bd issues filed for each deferred embed type with priority + linkage; PostCard implementation respects the decided scope.
close_reason: Merged in PR #36 (commit b41029c). Decision: PostCard v1 = text + app.bsky.embed.images only; deferred embeds degrade to Unsupported chip. Roadmap + 4 follow-on tickets (aku/6vq/xsu/umn) filed.

title: Rich-text renderer for AT Protocol post facets
description:
AT Protocol post records carry a facets array describing annotations on the post text:
- app.bsky.richtext.facet#mention (DID payload — resolve to handle for display, navigate to profile)
- app.bsky.richtext.facet#link (URI payload — open via custom tab or external browser)
- app.bsky.richtext.facet#tag (tag payload — navigate to search/hashtag feed)

Need a Compose-side renderer: (text: String, facets: List<Facet>, onClick: (FacetTarget) -> Unit) -> AnnotatedString. Style mentions/links/tags with primary color + appropriate semantics. ClickableText (or pointerInput w/ TextLayoutResult) for tap handling.

CRITICAL CORRECTNESS NOTE: facet byteStart/byteEnd are UTF-8 byte offsets, but Kotlin strings are UTF-16. Emoji, accented characters, and non-Latin text break any naive text.substring(byteStart, byteEnd) slice. The renderer must convert byte ranges to char ranges via a UTF-8 round-trip (text.toByteArray(Charsets.UTF_8).copyOfRange(...).toString(Charsets.UTF_8) approach, or a precomputed byte->char index map). Without this, the first emoji in a post breaks every facet boundary after it.

Lives in :designsystem (reused by feed, post detail, profile bio, notifications).

Blocks PostCard (nubecita-w0d).
acceptance_criteria: Composable renders String + List<Facet> as AnnotatedString with mention/link/tag styling; tap callbacks fire FacetTarget sealed type with payload; emoji-bearing test post (e.g. "hi 👋 @alice.bsky.social") renders mention boundary at correct char offset; unit test explicitly covers UTF-8<->UTF-16 boundary case.
notes: Upstream design discussion filed: kikin81/atproto-kotlin#20 (proposes a 3-tier API in two new artifacts at-protocol-compose + at-protocol-compose-material3). If the upstream helper ships in time, supersede this ticket and consume the SDK helper directly. If we need PostCard sooner, implement nubecita-side using the same shape (typed sealed parent + LinkAnnotation.Url + escape-hatch builder) so the migration to the upstream helper is a near-drop-in.
close_reason: Superseded by upstream atproto-kotlin v5.1.0 :compose-material3, adopted in PR #40 (commit 9f3e16d). Filed upstream as kikin81/atproto-kotlin#20 (closed/done) — instead of building a colocated facet renderer, PostCard will call rememberBlueskyAnnotatedString from the upstream lib.

title: Wire Coil for Compose image loading
description:
Coil is listed in CLAUDE.md as pending. Posts carry author avatars and (per the chosen embed scope) embedded images, neither of which can render without an image pipeline. coil-compose's AsyncImage is the Compose integration surface.

Decisions to make at implementation time:
- Where does the dep live? Default: add to :designsystem so every Compose image flows through one config (placeholder, error fallback, crossfade). Alt: standalone :core:image module if non-Compose call sites appear.
- Centralize an ImageLoader Hilt @Singleton with the chosen disk-cache size, OkHttp client, and crossfade defaults. Components consume a project-defined wrapper (e.g. NubecitaAsyncImage) rather than passing config per call site.
- Coil 3 vs 2: Coil 3 is current and KMP-friendly. Default to 3 unless a transitive blocks it.

Blocks PostCard (nubecita-w0d) — avatars and image embeds need this.
acceptance_criteria: coil-compose dep wired in :designsystem; ImageLoader provided as Hilt @Singleton; one canonical wrapper composable consumed by at least one component; placeholder/error states match Material 3 Expressive surface tokens.
close_reason: Merged in PR #35 (commit 4947525). coil-core + coil-network-okhttp wired in :app, coil-compose in :designsystem; ImageLoader Hilt @Singleton with 25% memory + 50MB disk cache; NubecitaApplication implements SingletonImageLoader.Factory; NubecitaAsyncImage + NubecitaAvatar primitives shipped.

title: Add DefaultAuthRepository unit tests covering refresh-on-success contract
description:
Per Copilot review on PR #34, DefaultAuthRepository's new behaviors aren't unit-tested:
- completeLogin success → SessionStateProvider.refresh() invoked
- signOut success → AtOAuth.logout() called + SessionStateProvider.refresh() invoked
- AtOAuth throws → Result.failure carrying the exception, NO refresh
- Log.e fires on failure (assertable via android.util.Log shadowing)

The block today: AtOAuth is a final concrete class from atproto-oauth — can't subclass for fakes. Two implementation paths:

(a) Ktor MockEngine: ~80 lines of test setup canned-responding to AtOAuth's PAR + token endpoint requests. Exercises real AtOAuth code paths. testImplementation(io.ktor:ktor-client-mock).

(b) Test-only OAuthFlowOps interface seam in :core:auth wrapping the three AtOAuth methods we use (beginLogin/completeLogin/logout). Production binding wraps real AtOAuth; tests use a fake. ~20 lines refactor + 30 lines test.

Lean toward (a) — keeps DefaultAuthRepository's current shape, tests via the seam Bluesky's actual auth server provides (HTTP). Decide at implementation time.

Also add coverage for DefaultAuthRepository.beginLogin which we wired up under nubecita-4g7 but never tested either.

title: Adaptive two-pane list-detail layout via material3-adaptive
description: Once the home feed + PostCard + profile primitives exist, wire a window-size-class-aware list-detail layout using androidx.compose.material3.adaptive so tablets / foldables / landscape phones show feed + selected post/profile side-by-side, and compact widths collapse to single-pane navigation. Uses NavigationSuiteScaffold for primary nav adaptivity and ListDetailPaneScaffold (or TwoPaneScaffold) for the feed↔detail split. Scope is shell-level adaptive scaffolding; does not own theme tokens (those live in :designsystem per nubecita-mi3).
close_reason: Merged via PR #85 (commit bb98eb0)

title: Add Konsist architecture tests to guard repo conventions
description:
Adopt Konsist (com.lemonappdev:konsist:0.17.3, Apache-2.0, ~1.6k stars, Kotlin Foundation grant winner, JUnit-based) as a testImplementation dependency so codebase conventions are enforced as unit tests instead of living only in CLAUDE.md. Konsist reads the project's Kotlin AST and exposes a fluent API for asserting package layout, naming, annotations, and inter-layer dependencies.

CAVEATS
- Latest release is 0.17.3 (2024-12-08), ~15 months old as of 2026-04-22. Main branch has a 2026-01 commit, so maintenance continues — but version-drift risk is real. Revisit the choice at the 18-month mark if no new releases land.
- Must run sonatype check on pkg:maven/com.lemonappdev/konsist@0.17.3 before the coord lands.
design:
Wiring
- Add konsist = "0.17.3" under [versions] and konsist = { module = "com.lemonappdev:konsist", version.ref = "konsist" } under [libraries] in gradle/libs.versions.toml.
- Add testImplementation(libs.konsist) to app/build.gradle.kts.
- Place rules under app/src/test/java/net/kikin/nubecita/konsist/ so the existing testDebugUnitTest task picks them up automatically — no new Gradle task, no new source set. Keep the package name literally 'konsist' so scanning excludes are easy (Konsist can skip its own test files via withoutPackage).

Starter rule set — encode conventions already documented in CLAUDE.md and the dependency-injection / mvi-foundation specs
1. ViewModelRulesTest — every class whose name ends in 'ViewModel' extends MviViewModel or androidx.lifecycle.ViewModel; every @HiltViewModel uses @Inject constructor(...); no field injection anywhere.
2. MviFoundationRulesTest — classes implementing UiState are data classes; UiEvent and UiEffect implementations are sealed interfaces / sealed classes; every VM exposes a StateFlow<*> property named 'state' (or inherited from MviViewModel).
3. PackageLayoutRulesTest — classes ending in 'ViewModel', 'Screen', 'State', 'Event', 'Effect' live under net.kikin.nubecita.ui..; @Module / @InstallIn classes live under net.kikin.nubecita.data.. (until a dedicated di/ package is introduced).
4. HiltRulesTest — every @Module is an object (matching the AtProtoModule / DataModule pattern) or abstract class (for @Binds); @Module classes are annotated @InstallIn.
5. ComposableRulesTest — functions annotated @Composable that take a ViewModel parameter obtain it via hiltViewModel() from androidx.hilt.navigation.compose, not androidx.lifecycle.viewmodel.compose.viewModel().

Non-goals for v1
- No clean-architecture layer assertion (domain/data/presentation) — the project isn't structured that way yet; revisit once feature modules land.
- No detekt / ktlint duplication — spotless+ktlint already cover formatting; Konsist stays in the 'architectural invariants' lane.
- No enforcement of immutable-list types on UiState fields — that's a Compose perf concern Konsist can check via type-name matching, but the kotlinx-collections-immutable API isn't rigidly named; defer until MVI base has ≥3 consumers to generalize the rule shape.
- No Konsist CI-only gating — tests run in the same testDebugUnitTest invocation the existing unit tests use; CI already covers that path.
acceptance_criteria:
- gradle/libs.versions.toml contains konsist version + library alias; app/build.gradle.kts declares testImplementation(libs.konsist).
- Sonatype check on pkg:maven/com.lemonappdev/konsist@0.17.3 is clean (not malicious, not end-of-life, policy compliant); flagged to user if not.
- At least 5 Konsist test classes under app/src/test/java/net/kikin/nubecita/konsist/ covering the rule groups listed in design.
- ./gradlew :app:testDebugUnitTest passes, with the Konsist tests included in the run (grep the test report for 'konsist' package output).
- ./gradlew spotlessCheck sortDependencies lint stays clean.
notes:
Starter projects: https://github.com/LemonAppDev/konsist/tree/main/samples/starter-projects
Docs: https://docs.konsist.lemonappdev.com/
Snippets: https://docs.konsist.lemonappdev.com/inspiration/snippets
close_reason: abandoned: upstream maintenance stalled — see LemonAppDev/konsist#1856 (December 2025 'is this still maintained?' discussion, no maintainer response 4 months later) and the 15-month gap since the 0.17.3 release (2024-12-08). PR #24 closed unmerged. Future convention-enforcement work will evaluate detekt (+ ForbiddenImport / custom rules) and/or ArchUnit-Kotlin when multi-module splits arrive.

title: Android instrumented tests via ReactiveCircus/android-emulator-runner
description: Add a GitHub Actions workflow that runs the app/src/androidTest/ source set on an emulator using ReactiveCircus/android-emulator-runner. Triggers (must NOT run on every PR — instrumented tests are slow and resource-heavy): (1) push to main, (2) workflow_dispatch for manual runs, (3) PR label-gated (e.g., run-instrumented) so reviewers can opt in when the risk warrants it. First payload: migrate the existing MainScreenTest.kt under this gate, and add a @HiltAndroidTest that @Injects XrpcClient and calls IdentityService.resolveHandle(bsky.app) end-to-end — satisfies the deferred 'live @Inject' half of nubecita-ipa's original acceptance criterion. Design context in docs/superpowers/specs/2026-04-22-atproto-hilt-module-design.md.
notes:
Add splash + auth-gated routing instrumented scenarios (added under add-splash-routing):
- Cold-start with no session: app launches, system splash visible, lands on Login (not Main).
- Cold-start with session present: seed the OAuthSessionStore via test setup, launch app, splash visible, lands on Main.
- Rotation during splash: rotate device while SessionStateProvider is still Loading; verify no crash, no flicker, lands on correct destination after rotation.
- Mid-session signOut: simulate AuthRepository.signOut() success while on Main; verify SessionStateProvider transitions to SignedOut and MainActivity reactively replaceTo's Login.
- Cold-start with corrupted store: tamper with the encrypted DataStore file; verify load() returns null, splash routes to Login (not crash).
close_reason: Merged via PR #75 (commits e79e314 + 157e7d4 + 1f2ef74 + 74cdc6f). Local + CI emulator both pass: 4 instrumented tests (3 FirebaseInitTest + 1 MainShellPersistenceTest).

Add a pre-push stage to .pre-commit-config.yaml that runs :app:compileDebugScreenshotTestSources so compile breakage is caught locally before the remote round-trip.
